README.html.title: Atom | Atom
README.html.summary: Atom Atom is an opinionated, type-safe build automation framework for .NET. It enables you to define your build logic in C#, debug it like standard code, and automatically generate CI/CD configuration files for GitHub Actions and Azure DevOps. Why Atom? Zero Context Switching: Write build logic in C# alongside your application code. Intellisense & Debugging: Step through your build process using your IDE. CI/CD Agnostic: Define logic once; Atom generates the YAML for GitHub and Azure DevOps. Modular: Pull in capabilities via NuGet packages (GitVersion, Azure KeyVault, etc.). Source Generators: Reduces boilerplate by automatically discovering targets and parameters. Basic Example Create a new file Build.cs #:package DecSm.Atom@2.* [BuildDefinition] [GenerateEntryPoint] partial class Build : BuildDefinition { Target SayHello => t => t .Executes(() => Logger.LogInformation("Hello, World!")); } Execute dotnet run Build.cs SayHello 25-12-16 +10:00 DecSm.Atom.Build.BuildExecutor: 22:46:01.754 INF Executing build SayHello 25-12-16 +10:00 SayHello | Build: 22:46:01.790 INF Hello, World! Build Summary SayHello │ Succeeded │ <0.01s Documentation Getting Started Introduction — What Atom is and why you'd use it Your First Build — Create a minimal build and run it Base vs Workflow Build — BuildDefinition vs WorkflowBuildDefinition Core Concepts Build Definitions — The [BuildDefinition] attribute and source generators Targets — Defining targets with the fluent TargetDefinition API Parameters — Declaring, requiring, and resolving parameters Secrets — Secure parameter handling with ISecretsProvider Artifacts — Producing and consuming build artifacts Variables — Sharing data between targets with workflow variables File System — IAtomFileSystem, RootedPath, and path providers Process Runner — Executing external processes Build Info — Build ID, version, and timestamp providers Build Options — Configuring build behaviour with IBuildOption Hosting — AtomHost, [GenerateEntryPoint], and host configuration Lifecycle Hooks — IAtomLifecycleHook for pre/post-build logic Logging & Reports — Spectre console output and report data File Transformations — Temporary, reversible file edits with TransformFileScope Workflows Overview — What workflows add on top of a base build Workflow Definitions — WorkflowDefinition, targets, and types Triggers — Push, pull request, and manual triggers Workflow Options — Checkout, deployment, conditions, and more Variables in Workflows — Cross-job data sharing Debugging Workflows — Local workflow simulation Modules Overview — What a module is and how to add one .NET — DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet GitHub Workflows — DecSm.Atom.Module.GithubWorkflows DevOps Workflows — DecSm.Atom.Module.DevopsWorkflows Azure Key Vault — DecSm.Atom.Module.AzureKeyVault Azure Storage — DecSm.Atom.Module.AzureStorage GitVersion — DecSm.Atom.Module.GitVersion Built-in Targets SetupBuildInfo — Initialises build ID, version, and timestamp ValidateBuild — Checks the build for common issues GenerateWorkflowFiles — Generates CI/CD YAML files Developer Guide Writing a Module — Creating a reusable Atom module NuGet package Custom Providers — Implementing IArtifactProvider, ISecretsProvider, and more Source Generators — How the Atom analysers and source generators work Testing — Using DecSm.Atom.TestUtils Reference CLI — Command-line arguments and the Atom global tool API Reference — Auto-generated API documentation (via DocFX) AI Disclaimer The Atom libraries are human-made, however GitHub Copilot completions have been used on a superficial level. Generative AI was also used to assist in writing tests and documentation. License Atom is released under the MIT License.

api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Args.CommandLineArg.title: Class CommandLineArgs | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Args.CommandLineArg.summary: Class CommandLineArgs Namespace DecSm.Atom.Build.Args Assembly DecSm.Atom.Build.dll Contains the parsed command-line arguments for an Atom build execution, providing a structured representation of user input. [PublicAPI] public sealed record CommandLineArgs Inheritance object CommandLineArgs Remarks An instance of this record is created by the DecSm.Atom.Build.Args.CommandLineArgsParser after parsing the raw string arguments provided to the Atom application. Constructors CommandLineArgs(bool, IEnumerable<IArg>) Contains the parsed command-line arguments for an Atom build execution, providing a structured representation of user input. public CommandLineArgs(bool IsValid, IEnumerable<IArg> Args) Parameters IsValid bool A value indicating whether the command-line arguments were parsed successfully and are valid. If false, the Atom application will typically terminate or display help. Args IEnumerable<IArg> A read-only list of IArg objects representing the individual arguments parsed from the command line. This list provides access to raw argument objects like HelpArg, CommandArg, or ParamArg. Remarks An instance of this record is created by the DecSm.Atom.Build.Args.CommandLineArgsParser after parsing the raw string arguments provided to the Atom application. Properties Args A read-only list of IArg objects representing the individual arguments parsed from the command line. This list provides access to raw argument objects like HelpArg, CommandArg, or ParamArg. public IReadOnlyList<IArg> Args { get; init; } Property Value IReadOnlyList<IArg> Commands Gets a read-only list of command arguments (CommandArg) from the command line. public IReadOnlyList<CommandArg> Commands { get; } Property Value IReadOnlyList<CommandArg> A read-only list of CommandArg objects. Remarks Each CommandArg represents a target to be executed. For atom Clean Build, this list would contain "Clean" and "Build". See Also CommandArg HasHeadless Gets a value indicating whether the headless argument (-hl or --headless) was provided. public bool HasHeadless { get; } Property Value bool true if the headless argument is present; otherwise, false. Remarks This mode is used for non-interactive environments like CI/CD pipelines, where user prompts should be disabled. Running atom MyCommand --headless or atom MyCommand -hl will result in this property being true. See Also HeadlessArg HasHelp Gets a value indicating whether the help argument (-h or --help) was provided. public bool HasHelp { get; } Property Value bool true if the help argument is present; otherwise, false. Remarks When this property is true, the Atom application should display help information and exit. Running atom --help or atom -h will result in this property being true. See Also HelpArg IHelpService HasInteractive Gets a value indicating whether the interactive argument (-i or --interactive) was provided. public bool HasInteractive { get; } Property Value bool true if the interactive argument is present; otherwise, false. Remarks This flag suggests that the Atom application may prompt the user for input if needed for certain parameters. Running atom MyCommand -i will result in this property being true. See Also InteractiveArg ParamService HasProject Gets a value indicating whether the project argument (-p or --project) was provided. public bool HasProject { get; } Property Value bool true if the project argument is present; otherwise, false. Remarks This argument allows specifying a custom Atom project directory, overriding the default. Running atom -p MyAtomProject will result in this property being true. See Also ProjectArg ProjectName HasSkip Gets a value indicating whether the skip argument (-s or --skip) was provided. public bool HasSkip { get; } Property Value bool true if the skip argument is present; otherwise, false. Remarks This flag instructs the build executor to skip the execution of dependent targets. Running atom MyTarget --skip or atom MyTarget -s will result in this property being true. See Also SkipArg BuildExecutor HasVerbose Gets a value indicating whether the verbose argument (-v or --verbose) was provided. public bool HasVerbose { get; } Property Value bool true if the verbose argument is present; otherwise, false. Remarks When true, the Atom application should output more detailed logging information for debugging or diagnostics. Running atom MyCommand --verbose or atom MyCommand -v will result in this property being true. See Also VerboseArg IsVerboseEnabled IsValid A value indicating whether the command-line arguments were parsed successfully and are valid. If false, the Atom application will typically terminate or display help. public bool IsValid { get; init; } Property Value bool Params Gets a read-only list of parameter arguments (ParamArg) from the command line. public IReadOnlyList<ParamArg> Params { get; } Property Value IReadOnlyList<ParamArg> A read-only list of ParamArg objects. Remarks Each ParamArg represents a key-value pair (e.g., --my-param value). For atom --param1 value1, this list would contain one ParamArg object. See Also ParamArg ProjectName Gets the project name specified by the project argument (-p or --project). public string ProjectName { get; } Property Value string The specified project name, or "_atom" if not provided. Remarks If the project argument is not provided, this defaults to "_atom". The Atom project typically resides in a directory with this name. If atom -p MyBuildScripts is run, this property will be "MyBuildScripts". See Also ProjectArg HasProject Methods GetValidationErrors() Gets a list of validation errors for the current command-line arguments. public IReadOnlyList<string> GetValidationErrors() Returns IReadOnlyList<string> A read-only list of error messages. An empty list indicates no validation errors.

api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Artifacts.IArtifact.title: Interface IArtifactProvider | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Artifacts.IArtifact.summary: Interface IArtifactProvider Namespace DecSm.Atom.Build.Artifacts Assembly DecSm.Atom.Build.dll Defines a provider for storing and retrieving build artifacts. [PublicAPI] public interface IArtifactProvider Remarks Implementations of this interface abstract the underlying storage mechanism, allowing for different backends such as cloud storage or local file systems. The Atom framework uses these providers to manage artifacts for targets like IStoreArtifact and IRetrieveArtifact. Properties RequiredParams Gets a list of parameter names required by the artifact provider to function correctly. IReadOnlyList<string> RequiredParams { get; } Property Value IReadOnlyList<string> Remarks These parameters typically include configuration settings or credentials needed to access the artifact storage. Methods Cleanup(IEnumerable<string>, CancellationToken) Deletes all artifacts associated with the specified build run identifiers. Task Cleanup(IEnumerable<string> runIdentifiers, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters runIdentifiers IEnumerable<string> A list of build run identifiers whose artifacts should be deleted. cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token to observe while waiting for the task to complete. Returns Task A System.Threading.Tasks.Task representing the asynchronous cleanup operation. Remarks This is useful for managing storage by removing old or temporary artifacts. GetStoredRunIdentifiers(string?, string?, CancellationToken) Retrieves a list of all stored run identifiers from the artifact storage. Task<IReadOnlyList<string>> GetStoredRunIdentifiers(string? artifactName = null, string? buildSlice = null, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters artifactName string An optional artifact name to filter the run identifiers by. buildSlice string An optional build slice to filter the run identifiers by. cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token to observe while waiting for the task to complete. Returns Task<IReadOnlyList<string>> A System.Threading.Tasks.Task<TResult> that completes with a read-only list of stored run identifiers. Remarks This method can be used to list available builds for retrieval or cleanup. RetrieveArtifacts(IEnumerable<string>, string?, string?, CancellationToken) Downloads one or more build artifacts from the configured storage. Task RetrieveArtifacts(IEnumerable<string> artifactNames, string? buildId = null, string? buildSlice = null, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters artifactNames IEnumerable<string> A list of artifact names to download. buildId string The unique identifier of the build from which to retrieve artifacts. If null, the provider may assume the current run or retrieve the latest version. buildSlice string An optional identifier for a specific build variation to retrieve artifacts from. cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token to observe while waiting for the task to complete. Returns Task A System.Threading.Tasks.Task representing the asynchronous download operation. StoreArtifacts(IEnumerable<string>, string?, string?, CancellationToken) Uploads one or more build artifacts to the configured storage. Task StoreArtifacts(IEnumerable<string> artifactNames, string? buildId = null, string? buildSlice = null, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters artifactNames IEnumerable<string> A list of artifact names to upload. Each name typically corresponds to a file or directory. buildId string The unique identifier for the current build. If null, the provider may use a default or environment-provided ID. buildSlice string An optional identifier for a specific build variation (e.g., in a matrix build) to associate the artifacts with. cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token to observe while waiting for the task to complete. Returns Task A System.Threading.Tasks.Task representing the asynchronous upload operation.

api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Artifacts.IAtomArti.title: Interface IAtomArtifactsParam | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Artifacts.IAtomArti.summary: Interface IAtomArtifactsParam Namespace DecSm.Atom.Build.Artifacts Assembly DecSm.Atom.Build.dll Provides a shared parameter for specifying artifact names. [PublicAPI] public interface IAtomArtifactsParam : IBuildAccessor Inherited Members IBuildAccessor.Services IBuildAccessor.Logger IBuildAccessor.AtomFileSystem IBuildAccessor.ProcessRunner IBuildAccessor.GetService<T>() IBuildAccessor.GetServices<T>() IBuildAccessor.GetParam<T>(Expression<Func<T>>, T, Func<string, T>) Remarks This interface defines the AtomArtifacts parameter, which is used by targets like IStoreArtifact and IRetrieveArtifact to identify which artifacts to process. The parameter is typically supplied via the --atom-artifacts command-line argument or an environment variable. Properties AtomArtifacts Gets the names of the artifacts to be processed. [ParamDefinition("atom-artifacts", "The name of the artifact/s to work with, use ',' to separate multiple artifacts", ParamSource.All, null)] string[] AtomArtifacts { get; } Property Value string[] Examples If the command-line argument --atom-artifacts "Package,Symbols" is provided, this property will return ["Package", "Symbols"]. Remarks This parameter specifies which artifacts to store or retrieve. Multiple names can be provided as a comma-separated string, which the framework parses into an array. If no value is provided, it defaults to an empty array.

api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Artifacts.IRetrieve.title: Interface IRetrieveArtifact | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Artifacts.IRetrieve.summary: Interface IRetrieveArtifact Namespace DecSm.Atom.Build.Artifacts Assembly DecSm.Atom.Build.dll Defines a target for retrieving build artifacts using an IArtifactProvider. [PublicAPI] public interface IRetrieveArtifact : IAtomArtifactsParam, ISetupBuildInfo, IBuildInfo, IVariablesHelper, IReportsHelper, IBuildAccessor Inherited Members IAtomArtifactsParam.AtomArtifacts ISetupBuildInfo.SetupBuildInfo IBuildInfo.BuildName IBuildInfo.BuildId IBuildInfo.BuildVersion IBuildInfo.BuildTimestamp IBuildInfo.BuildSlice IVariablesHelper.WriteVariable(string, string, CancellationToken) IReportsHelper.AddReportData(IReportData) IBuildAccessor.Services IBuildAccessor.Logger IBuildAccessor.AtomFileSystem IBuildAccessor.ProcessRunner IBuildAccessor.GetService<T>() IBuildAccessor.GetServices<T>() IBuildAccessor.GetParam<T>(Expression<Func<T>>, T, Func<string, T>) Remarks This interface, when implemented by a build definition, provides the RetrieveArtifact target. It uses the configured IArtifactProvider to download artifacts. Artifacts to be retrieved are specified via the AtomArtifacts parameter. Build information from ISetupBuildInfo (e.g., BuildName, BuildId) is used to identify the correct artifacts to download. This target is hidden by default, primarily for internal use or custom artifact workflows. Properties RetrieveArtifact Defines the target responsible for retrieving artifacts. Target RetrieveArtifact { get; } Property Value Target Remarks This target orchestrates the download of artifacts by invoking the RetrieveArtifacts(IEnumerable<string>, string?, string?, CancellationToken) method. It consumes the AtomArtifacts parameter to determine which artifacts to retrieve and utilizes build metadata from ISetupBuildInfo.

api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Artifacts.IStoreArt.title: Interface IStoreArtifact | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Artifacts.IStoreArt.summary: Interface IStoreArtifact Namespace DecSm.Atom.Build.Artifacts Assembly DecSm.Atom.Build.dll Defines a target for storing build artifacts using an IArtifactProvider. [PublicAPI] public interface IStoreArtifact : IAtomArtifactsParam, ISetupBuildInfo, IBuildInfo, IVariablesHelper, IReportsHelper, IBuildAccessor Inherited Members IAtomArtifactsParam.AtomArtifacts ISetupBuildInfo.SetupBuildInfo IBuildInfo.BuildName IBuildInfo.BuildId IBuildInfo.BuildVersion IBuildInfo.BuildTimestamp IBuildInfo.BuildSlice IVariablesHelper.WriteVariable(string, string, CancellationToken) IReportsHelper.AddReportData(IReportData) IBuildAccessor.Services IBuildAccessor.Logger IBuildAccessor.AtomFileSystem IBuildAccessor.ProcessRunner IBuildAccessor.GetService<T>() IBuildAccessor.GetServices<T>() IBuildAccessor.GetParam<T>(Expression<Func<T>>, T, Func<string, T>) Remarks This interface, when implemented by a build definition, provides the StoreArtifact target. It uses the configured IArtifactProvider to upload artifacts. Artifacts to be stored are specified via the AtomArtifacts parameter and are expected to be located in the AtomPublishDirectory. Build information from ISetupBuildInfo (e.g., BuildName, BuildId) is used to categorize or tag the artifacts. This target is hidden by default, primarily for internal use or custom artifact workflows. Properties StoreArtifact Defines the target responsible for storing artifacts. Target StoreArtifact { get; } Property Value Target Remarks This target orchestrates the upload of artifacts by invoking the StoreArtifacts(IEnumerable<string>, string?, string?, CancellationToken) method. It consumes the AtomArtifacts parameter to determine which artifacts to store and utilizes build metadata from ISetupBuildInfo.

api/DecSm.Atom.Build.AtomProjectData.htm.summary: Class AtomProjectData Namespace DecSm.Atom.Build Assembly DecSm.Atom.Build.dll [PublicAPI] public sealed record AtomProjectData Inheritance object AtomProjectData Properties IsFileBasedApp Whether the application is file-based (e.g., *.cs) or project-based (e.g., *.csproj). public required bool IsFileBasedApp { get; init; } Property Value bool ProjectName The name of the project, typically derived from the entry assembly. public required string ProjectName { get; init; } Property Value string

api/DecSm.Atom.Build.BuildInfo.IBuildIdP.title: Interface IBuildIdProvider | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Build.BuildInfo.IBuildIdP.summary: Interface IBuildIdProvider Namespace DecSm.Atom.Build.BuildInfo Assembly DecSm.Atom.Build.dll Defines a provider for generating a unique build identifier. [PublicAPI] public interface IBuildIdProvider Properties BuildId Gets the unique identifier for the current build. string BuildId { get; } Property Value string Remarks This ID should be consistent within a single build run but unique across different builds. If a build spans multiple jobs, this ID should be passed between them to ensure consistency. Methods GetBuildIdGroup(string) Returns an optional grouping key for a given build ID, which can be used to organize artifacts or logs. string? GetBuildIdGroup(string buildId) Parameters buildId string The build ID to generate a group for. Returns string A string representing the group, or null if no grouping is implemented. Examples A date-based build ID like "2024-01-15-..." could be grouped by month, returning "2024-01".

api/DecSm.Atom.Build.BuildInfo.IBuildInf.title: Interface IBuildInfo | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Build.BuildInfo.IBuildInf.summary: Interface IBuildInfo Namespace DecSm.Atom.Build.BuildInfo Assembly DecSm.Atom.Build.dll Provides centralized access to essential build metadata, such as version, name, and identifiers. [PublicAPI] public interface IBuildInfo : IBuildAccessor Inherited Members IBuildAccessor.Services IBuildAccessor.Logger IBuildAccessor.AtomFileSystem IBuildAccessor.ProcessRunner IBuildAccessor.GetService<T>() IBuildAccessor.GetServices<T>() IBuildAccessor.GetParam<T>(Expression<Func<T>>, T, Func<string, T>) Remarks This interface defines parameters that supply consistent build information throughout the execution lifecycle. For values that change over time (e.g., BuildTimestamp), it is recommended to consume them from ISetupBuildInfo. This ensures that a stable value is captured at the start of the workflow and reused across all targets, providing consistency. Properties BuildId Gets the unique identifier for the current build run. [ParamDefinition("build-id", "The unique identifier for the build run.", ParamSource.All, null)] string BuildId { get; } Property Value string Remarks This value can be overridden using the --build-id parameter. If not provided, it defaults to the value from the registered IBuildIdProvider. BuildName Gets the human-readable name of the build, derived from the solution or root directory name. [ParamDefinition("build-name", "The name of the build.", ParamSource.All, null)] string BuildName { get; } Property Value string Remarks This value can be overridden using the --build-name parameter. If not provided, it is automatically determined by searching for a .slnx or .sln file in the root directory and using its name. If no solution file is found, it defaults to the name of the root directory. BuildSlice Gets an optional identifier for a specific variation of a build, often used in CI/CD matrix jobs. [ParamDefinition("build-slice", "An identifier for a build variation, used in matrix jobs.", ParamSource.All, null)] string? BuildSlice { get; } Property Value string Remarks This value can be configured using the --build-slice parameter. It is useful for distinguishing between parallel build jobs (e.g., different target frameworks or operating systems). It is null if not specified. BuildTimestamp Gets the build timestamp as a Unix epoch timestamp (seconds). [ParamDefinition("build-timestamp", "The build timestamp in Unix epoch seconds.", ParamSource.All, null)] long BuildTimestamp { get; } Property Value long Remarks This value can be overridden using the --build-timestamp parameter. If not provided, it defaults to the timestamp from the registered IBuildTimestampProvider. Warning: This value may be inconsistent across different process instances. For a stable timestamp, consume this parameter from ISetupBuildInfo so that a consistent value is used across all targets. BuildVersion Gets the semantic version of the build. [ParamDefinition("build-version", "The semantic version of the build.", ParamSource.All, null)] SemVer BuildVersion { get; } Property Value SemVer Remarks This value can be overridden using the --build-version parameter. If not provided, it defaults to the version from the registered IBuildVersionProvider. The value is automatically parsed into a SemVer object.

api/DecSm.Atom.Build.BuildInfo.IBuildTim.summary: Interface IBuildTimestampProvider Namespace DecSm.Atom.Build.BuildInfo Assembly DecSm.Atom.Build.dll Defines a provider for determining the build timestamp. [PublicAPI] public interface IBuildOptionProvider Remarks Implementations receive the base options from Options and may return additional options to be merged in. Uses two-phase resolution: providers only receive the base options, not other providers' contributions, preventing inter-provider dependencies. Register implementations with DI as IBuildOptionProvider to have them automatically included. Methods GetBuildOptions(IReadOnlyList<IBuildOption>) Gets additional build options based on the current base options from IBuildDefinition. IReadOnlyList<IBuildOption> GetBuildOptions(IReadOnlyList<IBuildOption> baseOptions) Parameters baseOptions IReadOnlyList<IBuildOption> The base options from Options. Returns IReadOnlyList<IBuildOption> Additional build options to merge into the resolved set.

[PublicAPI] public abstract class BuildDefinition : IBuildDefinition Inheritance object BuildDefinition Implements IBuildDefinition Derived WorkflowBuildDefinition Examples A typical build definition: [BuildDefinition] internal partial class MyBuild : BuildDefinition, IMyTargets { // ... } Remarks The TargetDefinitions and ParamDefinitions properties are populated by source generators based on the interfaces and attributes used in the derived class. Constructors BuildDefinition(IServiceProvider) The standard abstract base class for creating build definitions, providing default implementations for IBuildDefinition. protected BuildDefinition(IServiceProvider services) Parameters services IServiceProvider The service provider for dependency injection. api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Definition.Generate.title: Class GenerateInterfaceMembersAttribute | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Definition.Generate.summary: Class GenerateInterfaceMembersAttribute Namespace DecSm.Atom.Build.Definition Assembly DecSm.Atom.Build.dll Triggers source generation to implement members from interfaces applied to a class. [PublicAPI] [AttributeUsage(AttributeTargets.Class)] public sealed class GenerateInterfaceMembersAttribute : Attribute Inheritance object Attribute GenerateInterfaceMembersAttribute Examples [GenerateInterfaceMembers] public partial class MyBuild : BuildDefinition, IMyTargets, IYourTargets { // The source generator will automatically implement all members // from IMyTargets and IYourTargets. } Remarks When this attribute is applied to a partial class, the source generator scans all interfaces implemented by the class. For each interface, it generates explicit implementations of its members, delegating the calls to the base class. This is primarily used to avoid boilerplate code when a build definition implements many target interfaces.

api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Definition.Generate.title: Class GenerateSolutionModelAttribute | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Definition.Generate.summary: Class GenerateSolutionModelAttribute Namespace DecSm.Atom.Build.Definition Assembly DecSm.Atom.Build.dll Triggers the source generation of a solution model based on a .sln or .slnx file. [PublicAPI] [AttributeUsage(AttributeTargets.Class)] public sealed class GenerateSolutionModelAttribute : Attribute Inheritance object Attribute GenerateSolutionModelAttribute Examples [BuildDefinition] [GenerateSolutionModel] public partial class MyBuild : BuildDefinition { // The generated solution model can now be accessed, e.g., `Solution.MyProject`. } Remarks When this attribute is applied to a partial class, the source generator searches for a solution file in the project's root directory. It then generates a C# model representing the solution's structure, including projects and their dependencies, making it available for use within the build definition.

api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Exceptions.AtomExce.summary: Class AtomException Namespace DecSm.Atom.Build.Exceptions Assembly DecSm.Atom.Build.dll Represents the base exception class for all Atom framework-specific exceptions. api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Exceptions.BuildCon.title: Class BuildConfigurationException | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Exceptions.BuildCon.summary: Class BuildConfigurationException Namespace DecSm.Atom.Build.Exceptions Assembly DecSm.Atom.Build.dll Thrown when the build configuration contains errors such as duplicate targets, missing dependencies, or circular dependencies. [PublicAPI] [Serializable] public class BuildConfigurationException : AtomException Inheritance object Exception AtomException BuildConfigurationException Examples Example 1: Duplicate target error with ListReportData throw new BuildConfigurationException("One or more targets are defined multiple times.") { ReportData = new ListReportData(new[] { "Target 'Build' is defined multiple times" }) { Title = "Duplicate Targets" } }; Example 2: Circular dependency error with TextReportData throw new BuildConfigurationException("Circular dependency detected: Build -> Test -> Build") { ReportData = new TextReportData("Dependency cycle:\n Build -> Test -> Build") { Title = "Circular Dependency Detected" } }; Example 3: Missing dependency error throw new BuildConfigurationException("Target 'Build' depends on target 'Compile' which does not exist."); Example 4: How to catch and handle try { var buildModel = buildResolver.Resolve(); } catch (BuildConfigurationException ex) { Console.WriteLine($"Configuration error: {ex.Message}"); if (ex.ReportData is not null) { reportService.AddReportData(ex.ReportData); } } Remarks This exception is thrown during build model resolution when the DecSm.Atom.Build.BuildResolver detects configuration errors that prevent the build from proceeding. Common scenarios include: Duplicate target names defined in the build A target depends on a non-existent target Circular dependencies between targets The exception may include a ReportData property with enhanced error details that can be rendered for better visualization of the configuration problem. Constructors BuildConfigurationException() Initializes a new instance of the BuildConfigurationException class. public BuildConfigurationException() BuildConfigurationException(string) Initializes a new instance of the BuildConfigurationException class with a specified error message. public BuildConfigurationException(string message) Parameters message string The message that describes the error. BuildConfigurationException(string, Exception) Initializes a new instance of the BuildConfigurationException class with a specified error message and a reference to the inner exception that is the cause of this exception. public BuildConfigurationException(string message, Exception innerException) Parameters message string The error message that explains the reason for the exception. innerException Exception The exception that is the cause of the current exception, or null if no inner exception is specified. Properties ReportData Gets optional enhanced error details that can be rendered to provide better visualization of the configuration problem. public ICustomReportData? ReportData { get; init; } Property Value ICustomReportData Remarks This property can contain ListReportData for listing multiple issues, or TextReportData for formatted text output, or any other ICustomReportData implementation. See Also AtomException BuildResolver

api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Exceptions.CommandL.title: Class CommandLineException | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Exceptions.CommandL.summary: Class CommandLineException Namespace DecSm.Atom.Build.Exceptions Assembly DecSm.Atom.Build.dll Thrown when command-line arguments are invalid or malformed. [PublicAPI] [Serializable] public class CommandLineException : AtomException Inheritance object Exception AtomException CommandLineException Examples Example 1: Missing parameter value throw new CommandLineException("Missing value for --project option. Usage: --project <path>") { ArgumentName = "project" }; Example 2: Invalid argument syntax throw new CommandLineException("Missing value for parameter 'output'. The next argument '--verbose' looks like another option. Usage: --output <value>") { ArgumentName = "output" }; Example 3: How to catch and provide user guidance try { var args = commandLineArgsParser.Parse(commandLineArgs); } catch (CommandLineException ex) { Console.WriteLine($"Invalid arguments: {ex.Message}"); if (ex.ArgumentName is not null) { Console.WriteLine($"Problematic argument: {ex.ArgumentName}"); } Console.WriteLine("Run with --help for usage information."); Environment.ExitCode = 2; } Remarks This exception is thrown when the command-line argument parser detects invalid or malformed arguments. Common scenarios include: Missing parameter values (e.g., --project without a path) Invalid argument syntax (e.g., parameter value that looks like another option) Unknown parameters or flags The ArgumentName property, when set, identifies the specific command-line argument that caused the error, making it easier to diagnose and fix the issue. For help with command-line usage, run the build with the --help flag. Constructors CommandLineException() Initializes a new instance of the CommandLineException class. public CommandLineException() CommandLineException(string) Initializes a new instance of the CommandLineException class with a specified error message. public CommandLineException(string message) Parameters message string The message that describes the error. CommandLineException(string, Exception) Initializes a new instance of the CommandLineException class with a specified error message and a reference to the inner exception that is the cause of this exception. public CommandLineException(string message, Exception innerException) Parameters message string The error message that explains the reason for the exception. innerException Exception The exception that is the cause of the current exception, or null if no inner exception is specified. Properties ArgumentName Gets the name of the command-line argument that caused the error, if applicable. public string? ArgumentName { get; init; } Property Value string Remarks This property helps identify which specific argument was problematic, making it easier to diagnose and fix command-line issues. For example, if the --project option is missing its value, this property would be set to "project". See Also AtomException CommandLineArgsParser

api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Exceptions.StepFail.title: Class StepFailedException | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Exceptions.StepFail.summary: Class StepFailedException Namespace DecSm.Atom.Build.Exceptions Assembly DecSm.Atom.Build.dll Represents an exception thrown when a step within an Atom build target fails. This exception provides additional reporting capabilities through custom report data. [PublicAPI] public sealed class StepFailedException : AtomException Inheritance object Exception AtomException StepFailedException Examples throw new StepFailedException("Could not validate a value"); try { ... } catch (Exception ex) { // It's not recommended to throw a new exception in a catch block without // including the original exception. throw new StepFailedException("An error occurred during the step execution", ex) { ReportData = new TextReportData(...); }; } Remarks This exception is used throughout the Atom framework to signal failures in build targets, such as task execution errors, failed external process calls (via IProcessRunner), or unmet validation criteria within a target's logic. Consumers of the Atom framework can also throw StepFailedException to programmatically halt a build target and optionally provide custom report data using the ReportData property. The ReportData property allows attaching custom reporting information that can be used for enhanced error reporting, logging, or debugging purposes by Atom's reporting services. This exception inherits from AtomException. Use BuildConfigurationException for configuration errors (duplicate targets, circular dependencies); use StepFailedException for runtime target execution failures. Constructors StepFailedException() Initializes a new instance of the StepFailedException class with an empty message. public StepFailedException() Remarks Using this constructor is generally not recommended. It is preferable to provide a descriptive message and/or an inner exception to aid in diagnosing the failure. StepFailedException(string?) Initializes a new instance of the StepFailedException class with a specified error message. public StepFailedException(string? message) Parameters message string The message that describes the error. If null, an empty string will be used. StepFailedException(string, Exception?) Represents an exception thrown when a step within an Atom build target fails. This exception provides additional reporting capabilities through custom report data. public StepFailedException(string message, Exception? innerException = null) Parameters message string innerException Exception Examples throw new StepFailedException("Could not validate a value"); try { ... } catch (Exception ex) { // It's not recommended to throw a new exception in a catch block without // including the original exception. throw new StepFailedException("An error occurred during the step execution", ex) { ReportData = new TextReportData(...); }; } Remarks This exception is used throughout the Atom framework to signal failures in build targets, such as task execution errors, failed external process calls (via IProcessRunner), or unmet validation criteria within a target's logic. Consumers of the Atom framework can also throw StepFailedException to programmatically halt a build target and optionally provide custom report data using the ReportData property. The ReportData property allows attaching custom reporting information that can be used for enhanced error reporting, logging, or debugging purposes by Atom's reporting services. This exception inherits from AtomException. Use BuildConfigurationException for configuration errors (duplicate targets, circular dependencies); use StepFailedException for runtime target execution failures. See Also AtomException BuildConfigurationException Properties ReportData Gets custom report data associated with this step failure. This property can be used to attach additional context, diagnostic information, or custom reporting data that helps with error analysis and reporting within the Atom framework. public ICustomReportData? ReportData { get; init; } Property Value ICustomReportData An ICustomReportData instance containing custom report information, or null if no custom report data is associated with this exception. Remarks This property can only be set during object initialization (init-only property). It's designed to provide extensible reporting capabilities for different types of step failures that can be processed by Atom's outcome reporters. See Also ListReportData TableReportData TextReportData See Also AtomException BuildConfigurationException

api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Hosting.ConfigureHo.title: Class ConfigureHostAttribute | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Hosting.ConfigureHo.summary: Class ConfigureHostAttribute Namespace DecSm.Atom.Build.Hosting Assembly DecSm.Atom.Build.dll Marks an interface as a source for host configuration, triggering source generation to apply its logic. [PublicAPI] [AttributeUsage(AttributeTargets.Interface)] public sealed class ConfigureHostAttribute : Attribute Inheritance object Attribute ConfigureHostAttribute Examples // 1. Define an interface with the attribute [ConfigureHost] public interface IMyHostConfigurator { void Configure(IHost host) { // Configuration logic here } } // 2. Implement the interface in your build definition [BuildDefinition] partial class Build : IMyHostConfigurator; // The source generator will now apply the Configure method during host setup. Remarks When an interface is decorated with this attribute, any methods it contains named Configure with a single Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting.IHost parameter will be automatically discovered. The source generator then implements ConfigureBuildHost(IHost) on the build definition, calling these methods to apply the specified configurations to the host after it is built.

Implement the interface in your build definition [BuildDefinition] partial class Build : IMyHostBuilderConfigurator; // The source generator will now apply the Configure method during host builder setup. api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Hosting.HostExtensi.summary: Class HostExtensions Namespace DecSm.Atom.Build.Hosting Assembly DecSm.Atom.Build.dll Provides extension methods for configuring and integrating Atom services into a .NET host. [PublicAPI] public static class HostExtensions Inheritance object HostExtensions Remarks This class simplifies the setup of an Atom application by registering essential services, logging providers, and dependencies required for the Atom framework to function correctly. Methods AddAtom<TBuilder, TBuild>(TBuilder, string[]) Configures the Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting.IHostApplicationBuilder with all necessary Atom services, dependencies, logging, and default settings. public static TBuilder AddAtom<TBuilder, TBuild>(this TBuilder builder, string[] args) where TBuilder : IHostApplicationBuilder where TBuild : BuildDefinition Parameters builder TBuilder The host application builder instance to configure. args string[] The command-line arguments provided to the application. Returns TBuilder The configured host application builder instance. Type Parameters TBuilder The type of the host application builder. TBuild The type of the build definition, which must implement BuildDefinition. Remarks This method performs the following key configurations: Registers the DecSm.Atom.Build.AtomService as a hosted service. Registers the build definition (TBuild) and related interfaces. Adds core Atom services like IParamService, ReportService, and Spectre.Console.IAnsiConsole. Registers default providers for build information: IBuildIdProvider, IBuildVersionProvider, and IBuildTimestampProvider. Configures file system access via IAtomFileSystem. Registers build execution and workflow generation services. Sets up logging with Spectre.Console and report providers, filtering out verbose Microsoft host logs. Parses command-line arguments and makes them available as CommandLineArgs. Resolves and registers the BuildModel. Invokes ConfigureBuildHostBuilder(IHostApplicationBuilder) if implemented by the build definition. UseAtom(IHost) Configures the built Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting.IHost instance for Atom-specific behaviors. public static IHost UseAtom(this IHost host) Parameters host IHost The Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting.IHost instance to configure. Returns IHost The configured Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting.IHost instance. Remarks This method invokes ConfigureBuildHost(IHost) if implemented by the build definition, allowing for post-build host configuration.

api/DecSm.Atom.Build.IAtomLifecycleHook..title: Interface IAtomLifecycleHook | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Build.IAtomLifecycleHook..summary: Interface IAtomLifecycleHook Namespace DecSm.Atom.Build Assembly DecSm.Atom.Build.dll Defines a hook that can participate in the Atom build lifecycle. [PublicAPI] public interface IAtomLifecycleHook Examples public class MyLifecycleHook : IAtomLifecycleHook { public Task BeforeExecute(CancellationToken cancellationToken) { // Check preconditions before targets are executed return Task.CompletedTask; } } Remarks Implementations of this interface can be registered in the dependency injection container to receive callbacks at specific points in the Atom build lifecycle. Multiple hooks can be registered and will be invoked in registration order. All methods have default no-op implementations, so implementers only need to override the methods they are interested in. Methods AfterExecute(CancellationToken) Called after all build targets have completed execution (regardless of success or failure). Task AfterExecute(CancellationToken cancellationToken) Parameters cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token. Returns Task A task representing the asynchronous operation. Remarks This hook is called in a finally-like manner, allowing modules to perform cleanup or post-execution processing. BeforeExecute(CancellationToken) Called after the build model has been resolved and before build targets are executed. Task BeforeExecute(CancellationToken cancellationToken) Parameters cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token. Returns Task A task representing the asynchronous operation. Remarks This is an ideal point for modules to perform validation or generate files that must be up-to-date before the build proceeds (e.g., workflow generation checks). Throwing an exception from this method will prevent the build from executing.

api/DecSm.Atom.Build.ISetupBuildInfo.htm.title: Interface ISetupBuildInfo | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Build.ISetupBuildInfo.htm.summary:
Interface ISetupBuildInfo Namespace DecSm.Atom.Build Assembly DecSm.Atom.Build.dll See SetupBuildInfo [PublicAPI] public interface ISetupBuildInfo : IBuildInfo, IVariablesHelper, IReportsHelper, IBuildAccessor Inherited Members IBuildInfo.BuildName IBuildInfo.BuildId IBuildInfo.BuildVersion IBuildInfo.BuildTimestamp IBuildInfo.BuildSlice IVariablesHelper.WriteVariable(string, string, CancellationToken) IReportsHelper.AddReportData(IReportData) IBuildAccessor.Services IBuildAccessor.Logger IBuildAccessor.AtomFileSystem IBuildAccessor.ProcessRunner IBuildAccessor.GetService() IBuildAccessor.GetServices() IBuildAccessor.GetParam(Expression>, T, Func) Properties SetupBuildInfo Gets the build target responsible for setting up the build ID, version, and timestamp. Target SetupBuildInfo { get; } Property Value Target A Target configured to initialize build information variables and report data. Examples While SetupBuildInfo is often implicitly part of a default build definition, here's how you might conceptualize its use and the consumption of variables it produces. Assume BuildName is set in the build definition (e.g., by inheriting from IBuildInfo). // In your Atom build definition (e.g., Build.cs) public class MyBuild : BuildDefinition, ISetupBuildInfo { // ISetupBuildInfo members (BuildIdProvider, etc.) would be implicitly available // or explicitly implemented if not using a base class that provides them. // The BuildName property is inherited from IBuildInfo public override string BuildName => "MyAwesomeProject"; // A custom target that depends on SetupBuildInfo having run Target Package => t => t .DependsOn(nameof(ISetupBuildInfo.SetupBuildInfo)) // Ensures build info is set up .Executes(async () => { // Retrieve variables set by SetupBuildInfo var buildId = await GetVariable("BuildId"); var buildVersion = await GetVariable("BuildVersion"); var buildTimestamp = await GetVariable("BuildTimestamp"); // Log or use these variables LogInformation($"Packaging {BuildName} version {buildVersion} (Build ID: {buildId}, Timestamp: {buildTimestamp})"); // ... further packaging logic ... }); // Main build flow Target Default => t => t.DependsOn(Package); } // To run this build (assuming Atom CLI): // atom Default Remarks The SetupBuildInfo target is a foundational step in the Atom build pipeline. It performs several key actions:
  • Retrieves the build name (from ) and writes it as a build variable named "BuildName".
  • Retrieves the build ID (from ) and writes it as a build variable named "BuildId".
  • Retrieves the build version (from ) and writes it as a build variable named "BuildVersion".
  • Retrieves the build timestamp (from ), converts it to a string, and writes it as a build variable named "BuildTimestamp".
  • Adds a "Run Information" section to the build report, displaying the build name, version, ID, and timestamp.
  • Logs the build ID, version, and timestamp using the configured logger for ISetupBuildInfo.
This target is marked as hidden (IsHidden()), meaning it's not typically intended for direct invocation by users but is expected to run as a dependency for other targets or as part of an initial setup sequence. It produces the following build variables, which can be consumed by subsequent targets in the build process:
  • BuildName The name of the build.
  • BuildId The unique identifier for the build execution.
  • BuildVersion The version of the software being built.
  • BuildTimestamp The timestamp of the build (as a string).
Successful execution relies on the correct registration and functioning of IBuildIdProvider, IBuildVersionProvider, and IBuildTimestampProvider services within the Atom framework. See Also BuildName WriteVariable(string, string, CancellationToken) AddReportData(IReportData)

[PublicAPI] public interface IValidateBuild : IReportsHelper, IBuildAccessor Inherited Members IReportsHelper.AddReportData(IReportData) IBuildAccessor.Services IBuildAccessor.Logger IBuildAccessor.AtomFileSystem IBuildAccessor.ProcessRunner IBuildAccessor.GetService<T>() IBuildAccessor.GetServices<T>() IBuildAccessor.GetParam<T>(Expression<Func<T>>, T, Func<string, T>) Remarks The primary purpose of IValidateBuild is to enhance the reliability and maintainability of Atom build scripts by catching common configuration mistakes early in the process. Properties ValidateBuild Gets the build target that performs validation checks on the Atom build. Target ValidateBuild { get; } Property Value Target Examples This target is included in BuildDefinition and is automatically available if the build inherits from that type. Otherwise, it can be manually included: public class Build : BuildDefinition { // ... other targets ... // The ValidateBuild target from IValidateBuild would be automatically available // and could be set as a dependency for other targets. Target MyCustomTarget => t => t .DependsOn(nameof(IValidateBuild.ValidateBuild)) // ValidateBuild is the property from IValidateBuild .Executes(() => { /* Do something after validation */ }); } The target can then be run with atom ValidateBuild Exceptions StepFailedException Thrown if the validation process encounters critical errors that prevent the build from proceeding. (Currently, this condition is not met as the 'errors' list is not populated).

[PublicAPI] public enum TargetRunState Fields Failed = 4 The target failed during execution. NotRun = 5 The target was not scheduled to run. PendingRun = 1 The target is scheduled to run but is waiting for its dependencies to complete. Running = 2 The target is currently executing. Skipped = 6 The target was scheduled to run but was skipped, typically due to a build failure or user option. Succeeded = 3 The target completed successfully. Uninitialized = 0 The target has not yet been initialized or processed by the build executor.

api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Params.IParamServic.summary: Interface IParamService Namespace DecSm.Atom.Build.Params Assembly DecSm.Atom.Build.dll Defines a service for resolving and managing build parameters from various sources. [PublicAPI] public interface IParamService Remarks The service resolves parameters from the following sources in order of precedence: Cache (if enabled) Command-line arguments Environment variables Configuration files (e.g., appsettings.json) Secret providers Interactive console prompts Default value This service is central to the framework's parameter handling, providing features for type conversion, secret masking, and caching. Methods CreateDefaultValuesOnlyScope() Creates a disposable scope that temporarily causes parameter resolution to be suppressed, returning only default values (when they exist). IDisposable CreateDefaultValuesOnlyScope() Returns IDisposable An System.IDisposable that restores the previous state upon disposal. CreateNoCacheScope() Creates a disposable scope within which parameter caching is temporarily disabled. IDisposable CreateNoCacheScope() Returns IDisposable An System.IDisposable that restores the previous cache state upon disposal. CreateOverrideSourcesScope(ParamSource) Creates a disposable scope that temporarily overrides parameter sources. IDisposable CreateOverrideSourcesScope(ParamSource sources) Parameters sources ParamSource The parameter sources to use within the scope. Returns IDisposable An System.IDisposable that restores the previous state upon disposal. GetParam(string, string?) Retrieves a string parameter value by its name. string? GetParam(string paramName, string? defaultValue = null) Parameters paramName string The name of the parameter to retrieve. defaultValue string The value to return if the parameter cannot be resolved. Returns string The resolved string parameter value, or the default value if not found. GetParam<T>(Expression<Func<T?>>, T?, Func<string?, T?>?) Retrieves a parameter value using a lambda expression to identify the parameter. T? GetParam<T>(Expression<Func<T?>> paramExpression, T? defaultValue = default, Func<string?, T?>? converter = null) Parameters paramExpression Expression<Func<T>> A lambda expression identifying the parameter (e.g., () => MyParam). defaultValue T The value to return if the parameter cannot be resolved. converter Func<string, T> An optional function to convert the resolved string value to the target type. Returns T The resolved parameter value, or the default value if not found. Type Parameters T The target type of the parameter. GetParam<T>(string, T?, Func<string?, T?>?) Retrieves a parameter value by its name. T? GetParam<T>(string paramName, T? defaultValue = default, Func<string?, T?>? converter = null) Parameters paramName string The name of the parameter to retrieve. defaultValue T The value to return if the parameter cannot be resolved. converter Func<string, T> An optional function to convert the resolved string value to the target type. Returns T The resolved parameter value, or the default value if not found. Type Parameters T The target type of the parameter. MaskMatchingSecrets(string) Replaces known secret values in the provided text with a mask (e.g. "*****"). string MaskMatchingSecrets(string text) Parameters text string The text to scan and mask. Returns string The text with any resolved secret values replaced by a mask.

api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Params.ParamSource..summary: Enum ParamSource Namespace DecSm.Atom.Build.Params Assembly DecSm.Atom.Build.dll Specifies the sources from which a parameter's value can be resolved. [PublicAPI] [Flags] public enum ParamSource Fields All = Cache | CommandLineArgs | EnvironmentVariables | Configuration | Secrets The parameter can be resolved from any of the available sources. Cache = 1 The parameter can be resolved from a local cache. CommandLineArgs = 2 The parameter can be resolved from command-line arguments. Configuration = 8 The parameter can be resolved from configuration files (e.g., appsettings.json). EnvironmentVariables = 4 The parameter can be resolved from environment variables. None = 0 No source is specified. Secrets = 16 The parameter can be resolved from a secret management system.

api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Secrets.IDotnetUser.title: Interface IDotnetUserSecrets | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Secrets.IDotnetUser.summary: Interface IDotnetUserSecrets Namespace DecSm.Atom.Build.Secrets Assembly DecSm.Atom.Build.dll An interface that, when implemented on a build definition, enables sourcing secrets from the .NET user secrets store. [PublicAPI] public interface IDotnetUserSecrets Examples // 1. Implement the interface in your build definition [BuildDefinition] partial class Build : IDotnetUserSecrets, IMyTarget; // 2. Define a secret parameter [SecretDefinition("my-secret", "A secret from user secrets.")] string MySecret => GetParam(() => MySecret); // The value for "my-secret" will now be resolved from the user secrets store. Remarks This interface uses source generation to automatically register the DecSm.Atom.Build.Secrets.DotnetUserSecretsProvider with the dependency injection container. This allows the IParamService to resolve secrets marked with SecretDefinitionAttribute from the local user secrets store, which is ideal for development-time secrets that should not be committed to source control. This interface is automatically included in the default BuildDefinition. Methods ConfigureBuilder(IHostApplicationBuilder) Configures the host builder to register the DecSm.Atom.Build.Secrets.DotnetUserSecretsProvider. [UsedImplicitly] protected static void ConfigureBuilder(IHostApplicationBuilder builder) Parameters builder IHostApplicationBuilder See Also DotnetUserSecretsProvider ISecretsProvider

api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Secrets.ISecretsPro.title: Interface ISecretsProvider | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Secrets.ISecretsPro.summary: Interface ISecretsProvider Namespace DecSm.Atom.Build.Secrets Assembly DecSm.Atom.Build.dll Defines a contract for retrieving sensitive configuration values (secrets) from a secure storage provider. [PublicAPI] public interface ISecretsProvider Examples // 1. Implement the interface public class MySecretsProvider : ISecretsProvider { public string? GetSecret(string key) => MySecureStore.Get(key); } // 2. Register the provider in your build definition protected override void ConfigureServices(IServiceCollection services) { services.AddSingleton<ISecretsProvider, MySecretsProvider>(); } // 3. Use a secret parameter in a target [SecretDefinition("my-api-key", "An API key.")] string MyApiKey => GetParam(() => MyApiKey); Remarks This interface is the core of the Atom framework's secret management system, enabling secure retrieval of sensitive data like API keys and connection strings. Implementations can integrate with various backends such as Azure Key Vault or .NET User Secrets. The framework automatically discovers and queries registered providers when resolving parameters marked with SecretDefinitionAttribute. Providers are queried in registration order, creating a fallback chain (e.g., from a cloud provider to local user secrets). Properties Priority Represents the execution or processing priority of the secrets provider. int Priority { get; } Property Value int Remarks A higher priority value indicates higher precedence when resolving secrets among multiple providers. The default value is 0. Methods GetSecret(string) Retrieves a secret value by its key. string? GetSecret(string key) Parameters key string The unique identifier for the secret to retrieve. Returns string The secret value associated with the key, or null if the secret is not found in this provider. Remarks Implementations should return null rather than throwing an exception if a key is not found, allowing the framework to query other registered providers. See Also SecretDefinitionAttribute IParamService

api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Util.TaskExtensions.summary: Class TaskExtensions Namespace DecSm.Atom.Build.Util Assembly DecSm.Atom.Build.dll Provides extension methods that add robust retry behavior to System.Threading.Tasks.Task and System.Threading.Tasks.Task<TResult> operations. [PublicAPI] public static class TaskExtensions Inheritance object TaskExtensions Methods WithRetry(Func<Task>, int, TimeSpan, CancellationToken) Retries an asynchronous operation produced by a factory when it faults. A new task is created for each attempt. public static Task WithRetry(this Func<Task> taskFactory, int retryCount = 5, TimeSpan retryDelay = default, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters taskFactory Func<Task> A delegate that creates a new System.Threading.Tasks.Task for each attempt. retryCount int The number of retries to attempt after the initial failure. retryDelay TimeSpan The delay between retry attempts. Defaults to 1 second if not specified. cancellationToken CancellationToken A token to observe before each attempt and during delays. Returns Task A task that completes when an attempt succeeds, or throws an System.AggregateException after all retries fail. Remarks Cancellation-related exceptions are immediately rethrown and do not trigger a retry. WithRetry(Task?, int, TimeSpan) Retries awaiting the provided System.Threading.Tasks.Task when it faults, up to a specified number of attempts. public static Task WithRetry(this Task? task, int retryCount = 5, TimeSpan retryDelay = default) Parameters task Task The task to await. retryCount int The number of retries to attempt after the initial failure. retryDelay TimeSpan The delay between retry attempts. Returns Task A task that completes when the underlying task succeeds, or throws an System.AggregateException after all retries fail. Remarks Cancellation-related exceptions are immediately rethrown and do not trigger a retry. WithRetry<T>(Func<Task<T>>, int, TimeSpan, CancellationToken) Retries an asynchronous operation produced by a factory when it faults. A new task is created for each attempt. public static Task<T> WithRetry<T>(this Func<Task<T>> taskFactory, int retryCount = 5, TimeSpan retryDelay = default, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters taskFactory Func<Task<T>> A delegate that creates a new System.Threading.Tasks.Task<TResult> for each attempt. retryCount int The number of retries to attempt after the initial failure. retryDelay TimeSpan The delay between retry attempts. Defaults to 1 second if not specified. cancellationToken CancellationToken A token to observe before each attempt and during delays. Returns Task<T> A task that completes with a result if successful, or throws an System.AggregateException after all retries fail. Type Parameters T The result type of the task. Remarks Cancellation-related exceptions are immediately rethrown and do not trigger a retry. WithRetry<T>(Task<T>?, int, TimeSpan) Retries awaiting the provided System.Threading.Tasks.Task<TResult> when it faults, up to a specified number of attempts. public static Task<T> WithRetry<T>(this Task<T>? task, int retryCount = 5, TimeSpan retryDelay = default) Parameters task Task<T> The task to await. retryCount int The number of retries to attempt after the initial failure. retryDelay TimeSpan The delay between retry attempts. Defaults to 1 second if not specified. Returns Task<T> A task that completes with the result if successful, or throws an System.AggregateException after all retries fail. Type Parameters T The result type of the task. Remarks Cancellation-related exceptions are immediately rethrown and do not trigger a retry.

api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Util.TypeUtil.html.title: Class TypeUtil | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Util.TypeUtil.html.summary: Class TypeUtil Namespace DecSm.Atom.Build.Util Assembly DecSm.Atom.Build.dll Provides flexible type conversion utilities, handling primitives, arrays, and generic collections. [PublicAPI] public static class TypeUtil Inheritance object TypeUtil Remarks This utility class is designed to handle complex type conversions beyond standard .NET capabilities, with special handling for arrays (from comma-separated strings) and generic collections. It supports custom converters for specialized types and is optimized for performance-sensitive scenarios like parameter resolution. Methods Convert<T>(string?, Func<string?, T?>?) Converts a string value to a specified target type, with optional custom conversion logic. [UnconditionalSuppressMessage("Trimming", "IL2026", Justification = "May fall back to the TypeDescriptor-based Convert(string, Type) helper (marked RequiresUnreferencedCode) when no custom converter, array/generic path, or AOT-safe TryConvert path can handle the input. This is a last-resort conversion; in trimmed/AOT builds callers should prefer passing explicit converters for unsupported types.")] public static T? Convert<T>(string? stringValue, Func<string?, T?>? converter) Parameters stringValue string The string value to convert. For array types, a comma-separated string is expected. converter Func<string, T> An optional custom conversion function that takes precedence over built-in logic. Returns T The converted value, or the default value for the type if conversion is not possible. Type Parameters T The target type to convert to. Remarks The method uses a prioritized strategy: custom converter, array conversion, generic collection conversion, and finally, standard System.ComponentModel.TypeDescriptor conversion. It is designed to be AOT-friendly by attempting simple, reflection-free conversions first.

api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Variables.IVariable.title: Interface IVariableProvider | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Variables.IVariable.summary: Interface IVariableProvider Namespace DecSm.Atom.Build.Variables Assembly DecSm.Atom.Build.dll Defines a provider for reading and writing workflow variables to a specific storage backend. [PublicAPI] public interface IVariableProvider Examples public class CustomVariableProvider : IWorkflowVariableProvider { public async Task<bool> WriteVariable(string variableName, string variableValue, CancellationToken cancellationToken) { // Custom logic to write to an external system return await WriteToExternalSystemAsync(variableName, variableValue); } public async Task<bool> ReadVariable(string jobName, string variableName, CancellationToken cancellationToken) { // Custom logic to read from an external system var value = await ReadFromExternalSystemAsync(jobName, variableName); return value != null; } } Remarks This interface is part of Atom's extensible variable management system. Multiple providers can be registered, and the IVariableService will delegate operations to them in a chain of responsibility. Implementations should return true if they successfully handle an operation, or false to allow the next provider in the chain to attempt it. Methods ReadVariable(string, string, CancellationToken) Reads a variable from the provider's storage system for a specific job context. Task<bool> ReadVariable(string jobName, string variableName, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters jobName string The name of the job context from which to read the variable. variableName string The name of the variable to read. cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token. Returns Task<bool> A task that resolves to true if the provider successfully found and retrieved the variable; otherwise, false. WriteVariable(string, string, CancellationToken) Writes a variable to the provider's storage system. Task<bool> WriteVariable(string variableName, string variableValue, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters variableName string The name of the variable to write. variableValue string The value to store for the variable. cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token. Returns Task<bool> A task that resolves to true if the provider successfully handled the write operation; otherwise, false to delegate to the next provider. See Also IVariableService

api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Variables.IVariable.title: Interface IVariableService | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Variables.IVariable.summary: Interface IVariableService Namespace DecSm.Atom.Build.Variables Assembly DecSm.Atom.Build.dll Defines a centralized service for managing workflow variables, coordinating operations across multiple providers. [PublicAPI] public interface IVariableService Remarks This service abstracts the complexity of multiple variable providers, offering a unified API for reading and writing variables. It delegates operations to registered IVariableProvider implementations, trying custom providers first before falling back to the base Atom provider. Methods ReadVariable(string, string, CancellationToken) Reads a variable from a specific job's context, making it available in the current execution context. Task ReadVariable(string jobName, string variableName, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters jobName string The name of the job context from which to read the variable. variableName string The name of the variable as defined in the build parameters. cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token. Returns Task A System.Threading.Tasks.Task representing the asynchronous read operation. Remarks This method enables cross-job variable sharing. It resolves the variable's argument name and attempts to read it using custom providers before falling back to the base Atom provider. WriteVariable(string, string, CancellationToken) Writes a variable to the workflow context, making it available for subsequent steps and jobs. Task WriteVariable(string variableName, string variableValue, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters variableName string The name of the variable as defined in the build parameters. variableValue string The value to store for the variable. cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token. Returns Task A System.Threading.Tasks.Task representing the asynchronous write operation. Remarks The service resolves the variable's argument name from the build definition and attempts to write it using custom providers first. If none succeed, it uses the base Atom provider.

api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Variables.IVariable.title: Interface IVariablesHelper | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Build.Variables.IVariable.summary: Interface IVariablesHelper Namespace DecSm.Atom.Build.Variables Assembly DecSm.Atom.Build.dll Provides helper methods for managing workflow variables within the Atom build system. [PublicAPI] public interface IVariablesHelper : IBuildAccessor Inherited Members IBuildAccessor.Services IBuildAccessor.Logger IBuildAccessor.AtomFileSystem IBuildAccessor.ProcessRunner IBuildAccessor.GetService<T>() IBuildAccessor.GetServices<T>() IBuildAccessor.GetParam<T>(Expression<Func<T>>, T, Func<string, T>) Examples // Writing variables in a target execution await WriteVariable("BuildId", "12345"); await WriteVariable("BuildVersion", "1.0.0"); Remarks This interface enables components to write variables that can be shared across different build steps and targets in the workflow execution context. Methods WriteVariable(string, string, CancellationToken) Writes a variable to the workflow context, making it available for later steps. Task WriteVariable(string name, string value, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters name string The name of the variable. value string The value of the variable. cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token. Returns Task A System.Threading.Tasks.Task representing the asynchronous operation. Remarks Variables written through this method are persisted in the current workflow execution context. If a variable with the same name already exists, it will be overwritten. See Also IBuildAccessor IVariableService
