api/DecSm.Atom.FileSystem.FunctionPathPr.title: Class FunctionPathProvider | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.FileSystem.FunctionPathPr.summary: Class FunctionPathProvider Namespace DecSm.Atom.FileSystem Assembly DecSm.Atom.FileSystem.dll A lightweight, delegate-based implementation of IPathProvider that resolves paths using a caller-supplied function rather than requiring a dedicated class. [PublicAPI] public sealed class FunctionPathProvider : IPathProvider Inheritance object FunctionPathProvider Implements IPathProvider Remarks Use FunctionPathProvider (or the ProvidePath extension in FileSystemHostExtensions) when path logic is simple enough to express inline rather than in a separate class. For complex resolution logic — such as searching the file system or combining multiple keys — a dedicated IPathProvider implementation is clearer. Properties Priority Gets the priority of this provider. public required int Priority { get; init; } Property Value int Remarks Higher values are queried before lower values. When two providers can both resolve the same key the one with the higher priority wins. Use a negative value to register a low-priority fallback that only applies when no other provider matches. Provider Gets the delegate that implements the path resolution logic. public required Func<string, RootedPath?> Provider { get; init; } Property Value Func<string, RootedPath> Remarks The delegate receives the key string and should return a RootedPath when it recognizes the key, or null to defer resolution to the next provider in the chain. Methods GetPath(string) Attempts to resolve a RootedPath for the given key. public RootedPath? GetPath(string key) Parameters key string The key identifying the path to locate (e.g. "Root", "Artifacts"). Returns RootedPath A RootedPath if this provider recognises key; otherwise null to pass resolution to the next provider in the chain.

api/DecSm.Atom.FileSystem.IAtomFileSyste.title: Interface IAtomFileSystem | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.FileSystem.IAtomFileSyste.summary: Interface IAtomFileSystem Namespace DecSm.Atom.FileSystem Assembly DecSm.Atom.FileSystem.dll Defines a specialised file system abstraction for Atom, layering build-aware path resolution on top of the standard System.IO.Abstractions.IFileSystem abstraction. [PublicAPI] public interface IAtomFileSystem Remarks Path resolution is intentionally separate from file I/O: GetPath(string) queries a prioritized chain of IPathProvider implementations and caches the result. Callers never need to hard-code paths or know which provider resolved a key. Properties CurrentDirectory Gets the current working directory of the application as a RootedPath. RootedPath CurrentDirectory { get; } Property Value RootedPath Remarks Evaluated eagerly each time the property is read — it is not cached because the working directory can change during the lifetime of the process. FileSystem Gets the underlying System.IO.Abstractions.IFileSystem instance used for all file I/O operations. IFileSystem FileSystem { get; } Property Value IFileSystem Remarks All System.IO.Abstractions.IFileSystem members on this interface delegate to this property so that consumers of IAtomFileSystem work against a single, consistent abstraction regardless of which concrete file-system backend is registered. Methods CreateRootedPath(string) Creates a new RootedPath bound to this file system instance from a raw string path. RootedPath CreateRootedPath(string path) Parameters path string The absolute string path. Returns RootedPath A new RootedPath associated with this file system instance. Remarks Prefer this factory method over constructing RootedPath directly so that the resulting path is automatically bound to this IAtomFileSystem rather than to the inner FileSystem. GetPath(string) Resolves a well-known path by key, querying the registered IPathProvider chain and caching the result for later calls. RootedPath GetPath(string key) Parameters key string An application-defined key that identifies the path (e.g. "Root", "Artifacts"). Keys are compared by the individual providers and are conventionally defined as constants. Returns RootedPath A RootedPath corresponding to the key. Remarks Results are cached after the first successful resolution so that repeated calls to GetPath("Root") always return the same RootedPath instance without re-querying providers. This also means that if two providers could resolve the same key, only the one with the highest priority (earliest in the ordered list) ever runs. Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown when no registered provider can resolve key, or when the resolution depth exceeds the circular-dependency guard threshold. GetPath<T>() Resolves the path for a given IPathMarker type by invoking its static Path(IFileSystem) method directly. RootedPath GetPath<T>() where T : IPathMarker Returns RootedPath A RootedPath for the specified marker type. Type Parameters T A type that implements IPathMarker. Remarks Unlike GetPath(string), this overload bypasses the provider chain and the cache entirely — it calls T.Path(this) on every invocation. Use it for paths that are statically determined by the marker type itself, such as source-generated project paths.

api/DecSm.Atom.FileSystem.IPathMarker.ht.title: Interface IPathMarker | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.FileSystem.IPathMarker.ht.summary: Interface IPathMarker Namespace DecSm.Atom.FileSystem Assembly DecSm.Atom.FileSystem.dll Defines a contract for types that represent a specific, well-known file path within the file system, resolved relative to the current System.IO.Abstractions.IFileSystem instance. [PublicAPI] public interface IPathMarker Remarks Implement this interface (typically as a nested type or a source-generated partial class) to give a strongly-typed name to a path that would otherwise be identified by a magic string. Unlike keys resolved through GetPath(string), IPathMarker paths are statically computed by the marker type itself via the Path(IFileSystem) static abstract method. Methods Path(IFileSystem) Computes the rooted path that this marker represents, evaluated against the given fileSystem. public static abstract RootedPath Path(IFileSystem fileSystem) Parameters fileSystem IFileSystem The file system instance to bind the path to. Passing the current IAtomFileSystem keeps the returned RootedPath consistent with all other paths in the build. Returns RootedPath A RootedPath representing the location of the file or directory.

api/DecSm.Atom.FileSystem.IPathProvider..title: Interface IPathProvider | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.FileSystem.IPathProvider..summary: Interface IPathProvider Namespace DecSm.Atom.FileSystem Assembly DecSm.Atom.FileSystem.dll Defines a provider that can resolve a RootedPath for a given key within the Atom file system. [PublicAPI] public interface IPathProvider Remarks Multiple providers can be registered simultaneously. GetPath(string) queries them in descending Priority order and returns the first non-null result. A provider that does not recognize a key should return null to let the next provider in the chain handle it. Because GetPath(string) caches successful resolutions, a provider's GetPath(string) method is called at most once per key per lifetime of the IAtomFileSystem instance. Properties Priority Gets the priority of this provider. int Priority { get; } Property Value int Remarks Higher values are queried before lower values. When two providers can both resolve the same key the one with the higher priority wins. Use a negative value to register a low-priority fallback that only applies when no other provider matches. Methods GetPath(string) Attempts to resolve a RootedPath for the given key. RootedPath? GetPath(string key) Parameters key string The key identifying the path to locate (e.g. "Root", "Artifacts"). Returns RootedPath A RootedPath if this provider recognises key; otherwise null to pass resolution to the next provider in the chain.

api/DecSm.Atom.FileSystem.RootedPath.htm.title: Class RootedPath | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.FileSystem.RootedPath.htm.summary: Class RootedPath Namespace DecSm.Atom.FileSystem Assembly DecSm.Atom.FileSystem.dll Represents a file system path that is rooted within a specific System.IO.Abstractions.IFileSystem instance. [PublicAPI] public record RootedPath Inheritance object RootedPath Remarks Properties such as FileExists, FileName, and DirectoryName intentionally return null when the path does not match the expected entry type (file vs. directory), rather than throwing, so callers can use null-coalescing patterns naturally. Constructors RootedPath(IFileSystem, string) Represents a file system path that is rooted within a specific System.IO.Abstractions.IFileSystem instance. public RootedPath(IFileSystem FileSystem, string Path) Parameters FileSystem IFileSystem The file system instance this path belongs to. Path string The absolute path string. Remarks Properties such as FileExists, FileName, and DirectoryName intentionally return null when the path does not match the expected entry type (file vs. directory), rather than throwing, so callers can use null-coalescing patterns naturally. Properties DirectoryExists Indicates whether the path exists in the file system as a directory. public bool DirectoryExists { get; } Property Value bool DirectoryName Gets the parent directory path of the current path as reported by the file system. public string? DirectoryName { get; } Property Value string The directory component of the path if the path resolves to an existing directory; otherwise null. Returns null (rather than throwing) when the path points to a file or does not exist. Remarks This delegates to System.IO.Abstractions.IPath.GetDirectoryName(string), which strips the last path segment and trailing separator — it does not return the directory's own name. FileExists Indicates whether the path exists in the file system as a file. public bool FileExists { get; } Property Value bool FileName Gets the file name (including extension) from the current path. public string? FileName { get; } Property Value string The file name if the path resolves to an existing file; otherwise null. Returns null (rather than throwing) when the path points to a directory or does not exist, so callers can safely use null-coalescing without a prior existence check. FileNameWithoutExtension Gets the file name of the current path without its extension. public string FileNameWithoutExtension { get; } Property Value string Remarks Unlike FileName, this property does not check whether the file exists — it is a pure path-string operation and always returns a value. FileSystem The file system instance this path belongs to. public IFileSystem FileSystem { get; init; } Property Value IFileSystem Parent Gets the parent directory of the current path. public RootedPath? Parent { get; } Property Value RootedPath A new RootedPath representing the parent directory, or null if the current path is already a root. Remarks A trailing directory separator on the input path is stripped before the parent is computed so that /foo/ and /foo both yield the same parent (/). The returned path always ends with System.IO.Abstractions.IPath.DirectorySeparatorChar so it can be combined further with the / operator without producing double separators. Path The absolute path string. public string Path { get; init; } Property Value string PathExists Indicates whether the path exists in the file system as either a file or a directory. public bool PathExists { get; } Property Value bool Remarks Equivalent to FileExists || DirectoryExists. Methods ToString() Returns a string that represents the current object. public override string ToString() Returns string A string that represents the current object. Operators operator /(RootedPath, string) Combines the current RootedPath with a relative string segment using System.IO.Abstractions.IPath.Combine(string, string). public static RootedPath operator /(RootedPath left, string right) Parameters left RootedPath The base RootedPath. right string The relative segment to append. Returns RootedPath A new RootedPath representing the combined path. Remarks Using an operator instead of a method keeps path-construction code concise and readable, e.g. atomFileSystem.GetPath("Root") / "src" / "MyProject". The operator preserves the original FileSystem reference so the resulting path remains bound to the same file-system abstraction. implicit operator string(RootedPath) Implicitly converts a RootedPath to its string representation. public static implicit operator string(RootedPath path) Parameters path RootedPath The RootedPath to convert. Returns string The string representation of the path. Remarks Allows a RootedPath to be passed directly to any API that accepts a string path without an explicit cast or .ToString() call.

api/DecSm.Atom.FileSystem.TransformFileS.title: Class TransformFileScope | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.FileSystem.TransformFileS.summary: Class TransformFileScope Namespace DecSm.Atom.FileSystem Assembly DecSm.Atom.FileSystem.dll Provides disposable scopes for performing temporary, reversible transformations on a single file's content. [PublicAPI] public sealed class TransformFileScope Inheritance object TransformFileScope Examples await using var scope = await TransformFileScope.CreateAsync( myProjectFile, content => content.Replace("1.0.0", "2.0.0")); // File now contains "2.0.0". Original is restored when scope is disposed. Remarks On creation the file's current content is captured. The supplied transform is applied and written back immediately. On disposal the original content is restored, or — if the file did not exist before the scope was opened — the file is deleted. This is useful during an operation when a file (e.g. a project file or a config file) needs to be temporarily modified for a specific step without permanently altering it. Call CancelRestore() before disposing to commit the transformation instead of rolling it back. Methods Add(Func<string, string>) Applies an additional transformation to the file within an already-open scope, using synchronous I/O. public TransformFileScope Add(Func<string, string> transform) Parameters transform Func<string, string> A function that receives the file's current (already-transformed) content and returns the next content to write. Returns TransformFileScope The same TransformFileScope instance, for fluent chaining. Remarks The scope still restores to the original content (captured at creation time), not the content at the point of the last Add call. Multiple calls build up transformations; disposal always unwinds them all at once. Exceptions ObjectDisposedException Thrown if the scope has already been disposed. AddAsync(Func<string, string>, CancellationToken) Applies an additional transformation to the file within an already-open scope, using asynchronous I/O. public Task<TransformFileScope> AddAsync(Func<string, string> transform, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters transform Func<string, string> A function that receives the file's current (already-transformed) content and returns the next content to write. cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token. Returns Task<TransformFileScope> The same TransformFileScope instance, for fluent chaining. Remarks The scope still restores to the original content (captured at creation time), not the content at the point of the last Add call. Multiple calls build up transformations; disposal always unwinds them all at once. Exceptions ObjectDisposedException Thrown if the scope has already been disposed. OperationCanceledException Thrown (and the file restored) if cancellationToken is cancelled during the write. CancelRestore() Prevents the file from being restored to its original content when the scope is disposed. public void CancelRestore() Remarks Call this when the transformation should be made permanent — for example after a build step has successfully consumed the transformed file and the change no longer needs to be rolled back. Once called it cannot be undone. Create(RootedPath, Func<string, string>) Creates a new TransformFileScope, applying a synchronous transformation to the target file. public static TransformFileScope Create(RootedPath file, Func<string, string> transform) Parameters file RootedPath The file to transform. transform Func<string, string> A function that receives the file's current content (or an empty string if the file does not yet exist) and returns the new content to write. Returns TransformFileScope A TransformFileScope that will restore the file when disposed. CreateAsync(RootedPath, Func<string, string>, CancellationToken) Creates a new TransformFileScope, applying an asynchronous transformation to the target file. public static Task<TransformFileScope> CreateAsync(RootedPath file, Func<string, string> transform, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters file RootedPath The file to transform. transform Func<string, string> A function that receives the file's current content (or an empty string if the file does not yet exist) and returns the new content to write. cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token. Returns Task<TransformFileScope> A TransformFileScope that will restore the file when disposed. Exceptions OperationCanceledException Thrown (and the file restored) if cancellationToken is cancelled while the transformed content is being written. Dispose() Synchronously disposes the scope and restores the file to its original state. public void Dispose() Remarks If CancelRestore() was called, the file is left as-is. If the file did not exist when the scope was created, it is deleted. Otherwise the original content is written back. Calling this method more than once is safe — subsequent calls are no-ops. No cancellation token is accepted here: we prefer to wait for the write to complete so the file is not left in a partially-written state. DisposeAsync() Asynchronously disposes the scope and restores the file to its original state. public ValueTask DisposeAsync() Returns ValueTask Remarks If CancelRestore() was called, the file is left as-is. If the file did not exist when the scope was created, it is deleted. Otherwise the original content is written back. Calling this method more than once is safe — subsequent calls are no-ops. No cancellation token is accepted here: we prefer to wait for the write to complete so the file is not left in a partially-written state.

api/DecSm.Atom.FileSystem.TransformFileS.title: Class TransformFileScopeExtensions | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.FileSystem.TransformFileS.summary: Class TransformFileScopeExtensions Namespace DecSm.Atom.FileSystem Assembly DecSm.Atom.FileSystem.dll Provides extension methods for fluently chaining additional transformations onto a System.Threading.Tasks.Task<TResult> that yields a scope returned by one of the static CreateAsync factory methods. [PublicAPI] public static class TransformFileScopeExtensions Inheritance object TransformFileScopeExtensions Remarks These extensions exist so that CreateAsync and one or more AddAsync calls can be written as a single expression without intermediate variables: await using var scope = await TransformFileScope .CreateAsync(file, _ => "v1") .AddAsync(c => c + "-patched"); Without them, the caller would need to await CreateAsync into a local and then await AddAsync separately. Methods AddAsync(Task<TransformFileScope>, Func<string, string>) Applies an additional asynchronous transformation to the file within the TransformFileScope returned by scopeTask. public static Task<TransformFileScope> AddAsync(this Task<TransformFileScope> scopeTask, Func<string, string> transform) Parameters scopeTask Task<TransformFileScope> A task that produces a TransformFileScope. transform Func<string, string> A function to apply to the file's current content. Returns Task<TransformFileScope> A task that completes with the same TransformFileScope instance after the transformation has been applied. AddAsync(Task<TransformMultiFileScope>, Func<string, string>) Applies an additional asynchronous transformation to each file within the TransformMultiFileScope returned by scopeTask. public static Task<TransformMultiFileScope> AddAsync(this Task<TransformMultiFileScope> scopeTask, Func<string, string> transform) Parameters scopeTask Task<TransformMultiFileScope> A task that produces a TransformMultiFileScope. transform Func<string, string> A function to apply to each file's current content. Returns Task<TransformMultiFileScope> A task that completes with the same TransformMultiFileScope instance after the transformation has been applied.

api/DecSm.Atom.FileSystem.TransformMulti.title: Class TransformMultiFileScope | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.FileSystem.TransformMulti.summary: Class TransformMultiFileScope Namespace DecSm.Atom.FileSystem Assembly DecSm.Atom.FileSystem.dll Provides disposable scopes for performing temporary, reversible transformations on multiple files simultaneously. Upon disposal, the original content of every managed file is restored unless restoration is explicitly cancelled. [PublicAPI] public sealed class TransformMultiFileScope Inheritance object TransformMultiFileScope Remarks The behaviour mirrors TransformFileScope but operates on a collection of files in parallel (via Task.WhenAll). Each file's original content is captured independently: files that did not exist before the scope was created are deleted on disposal, while existing files have their content written back. This is useful when a build step needs to simultaneously patch a set of related files — for example injecting a shared version number into multiple project files — and must guarantee that all files are restored even if the step fails partway through. Call CancelRestore() before disposing to commit all transformations permanently instead of rolling them back. Methods Add(Func<string, string>) Applies an additional synchronous transformation to every file within an already-open scope. public TransformMultiFileScope Add(Func<string, string> transform) Parameters transform Func<string, string> A function that receives each file's current (already-transformed) content and returns the next content to write. Returns TransformMultiFileScope The same TransformMultiFileScope instance, for fluent chaining. Remarks The scope always restores to the original contents captured at creation time, not the state after the most recent Add call. Exceptions ObjectDisposedException Thrown if the scope has already been disposed. AddAsync(Func<string, string>, CancellationToken) Applies an additional asynchronous transformation to every file within an already-open scope. public Task<TransformMultiFileScope> AddAsync(Func<string, string> transform, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters transform Func<string, string> A function that receives each file's current (already-transformed) content and returns the next content to write. cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token. Returns Task<TransformMultiFileScope> The same TransformMultiFileScope instance, for fluent chaining. Remarks The scope always restores to the original contents captured at creation time, not the state after the most recent Add call. Exceptions ObjectDisposedException Thrown if the scope has already been disposed. OperationCanceledException Thrown (and all files restored) if cancellationToken is cancelled during the write. CancelRestore() Prevents all files from being restored to their original content when the scope is disposed. public void CancelRestore() Remarks Call this when the transformation should be made permanent — for example after a build step has successfully consumed the transformed file and the change no longer needs to be rolled back. Once called it cannot be undone. Create(IEnumerable<RootedPath>, Func<string, string>) Creates a new TransformMultiFileScope, applying a synchronous transformation to each file in files. public static TransformMultiFileScope Create(IEnumerable<RootedPath> files, Func<string, string> transform) Parameters files IEnumerable<RootedPath> The files to transform. transform Func<string, string> A function applied to each file's current content (or an empty string for files that do not yet exist). Returns TransformMultiFileScope A TransformMultiFileScope that will restore all files when disposed. CreateAsync(IEnumerable<RootedPath>, Func<string, string>, CancellationToken) Creates a new TransformMultiFileScope, applying an asynchronous transformation to each file in files. public static Task<TransformMultiFileScope> CreateAsync(IEnumerable<RootedPath> files, Func<string, string> transform, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters files IEnumerable<RootedPath> The files to transform. transform Func<string, string> A function applied to each file's current content (or an empty string for files that do not yet exist). The same function is called for every file; if you need per-file logic consider multiple TransformFileScope instances instead. cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token. Returns Task<TransformMultiFileScope> A TransformMultiFileScope that will restore all files when disposed. Exceptions OperationCanceledException Thrown (and all files restored) if cancellationToken is cancelled while the transformed content is being written. Dispose() Synchronously disposes the scope and restores all managed files to their original state. public void Dispose() Remarks If CancelRestore() was called, all files are left as-is. Files that did not exist when the scope was created are deleted. All other files have their original content written back in parallel. Calling this method more than once is safe — subsequent calls are no-ops. No cancellation token is accepted: restoring files should always complete to avoid leaving the repository in a partially-modified state. DisposeAsync() Asynchronously disposes the scope and restores all managed files to their original state. public ValueTask DisposeAsync() Returns ValueTask Remarks If CancelRestore() was called, all files are left as-is. Files that did not exist when the scope was created are deleted. All other files have their original content written back in parallel. Calling this method more than once is safe — subsequent calls are no-ops. No cancellation token is accepted: restoring files should always complete to avoid leaving the repository in a partially-modified state.

api/DecSm.Atom.Module.AzureKeyVault.IAzu.summary: Interface IAzureKeyVault Namespace DecSm.Atom.Module.AzureKeyVault Assembly DecSm.Atom.Module.AzureKeyVault.dll Provides integration with Azure Key Vault for managing secrets and configuration. [PublicAPI] public interface IAzureKeyVault : IBuildAccessor Inherited Members IBuildAccessor.Services IBuildAccessor.Logger IBuildAccessor.AtomFileSystem IBuildAccessor.ProcessRunner IBuildAccessor.GetService<T>() IBuildAccessor.GetServices<T>() IBuildAccessor.GetParam<T>(Expression<Func<T>>, T, Func<string, T>) Remarks This interface defines the parameters required to connect to an Azure Key Vault and configures the necessary services for retrieving secrets. Properties AzureKeyVaultValueInjections Configures how Azure Key Vault parameter values are injected into the build process. AzureKeyVaultValueInjections AzureKeyVaultValueInjections { get; } Property Value AzureKeyVaultValueInjections Remarks This property allows customization of the source for each Azure Key Vault related parameter. By default, most parameters are expected from environment variables, while the app secret is expected from a secure secret store. AzureVaultAddress Gets the address (URI) of the Azure Key Vault. [ParamDefinition("azure-vault-address", "Address for the Azure Vault", ParamSource.All, null)] string? AzureVaultAddress { get; } Property Value string Remarks This parameter is crucial for identifying which Azure Key Vault instance to connect to. It can be provided via command-line arguments, environment variables, or appsettings.json. AzureVaultAppId Gets the Application (Client) ID for the App Registration that has access to the Azure Key Vault. [ParamDefinition("azure-vault-app-id", "Azure ID for App Registration that has access to the Azure Vault", ParamSource.All, null)] string? AzureVaultAppId { get; } Property Value string Remarks This ID is used for service principal authentication to Azure Key Vault. Ensure the registered application has appropriate permissions (e.g., Get, List) on secrets in the vault. AzureVaultAppSecret Gets the client secret for the App Registration that has access to the Azure Key Vault. [ParamDefinition("azure-vault-app-secret", "Azure Secret for App Registration that has access to the Azure Vault", ParamSource.All, null)] string? AzureVaultAppSecret { get; } Property Value string Remarks This secret is used in conjunction with the AzureVaultAppId for authenticating the application to Azure Key Vault. It should be treated as sensitive information. AzureVaultAuthPort Gets the port number for the local authentication redirect during the Azure Key Vault authentication process. [ParamDefinition("azure-vault-auth-port", "Port for local auth redirect (default 3421). Set to 0 to use a random port.", ParamSource.All, null)] int AzureVaultAuthPort { get; } Property Value int Remarks This port is used when performing device code or interactive authentication flows. The default port is 3421. Setting this to 0 will select a random available port. AzureVaultTenantId Gets the Tenant ID associated with the Azure Key Vault. [ParamDefinition("azure-vault-tenant-id", "Tenant ID for the Azure Vault", ParamSource.All, null)] string? AzureVaultTenantId { get; } Property Value string Remarks The Tenant ID is part of the authentication process to ensure access to the correct Azure Active Directory tenant. Methods ConfigureBuilder(IHostApplicationBuilder) Configures the host builder to add Azure Key Vault services. Remarks This method registers AzureKeySecretsProvider as a singleton service, making it available for secret retrieval and workflow option provisioning throughout the build.

api/DecSm.Atom.Module.DevopsWorkflows.De.title: Class Devops | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Module.DevopsWorkflows.De.summary: Class Devops Namespace DecSm.Atom.Module.DevopsWorkflows Assembly DecSm.Atom.Module.DevopsWorkflows.dll Provides utility methods and properties for interacting with Azure DevOps Pipelines. [PublicAPI] public static class Devops Inheritance object Devops Remarks This static class offers convenient access to Azure DevOps environment variables, and defines standard pipeline directories for artifact staging and binary output. Properties IsDevopsPipelines Gets a value indicating whether the current execution environment is Azure DevOps Pipelines. public static bool IsDevopsPipelines { get; } Property Value bool PipelineArtifactDirectory Gets the default pipeline artifact directory path for Azure DevOps Pipelines. public static string PipelineArtifactDirectory { get; } Property Value string Remarks This path typically corresponds to $(Build.ArtifactStagingDirectory) in Azure DevOps, used for staging artifacts before publishing them. PipelinePublishDirectory Gets the default pipeline publish directory path for Azure DevOps Pipelines. public static string PipelinePublishDirectory { get; } Property Value string Remarks This path typically corresponds to $(Build.BinariesDirectory) in Azure DevOps, where compiled binaries and other build outputs are placed. WorkflowType Gets an instance of DevopsWorkflowType for defining Azure DevOps-specific workflow types. public static DevopsWorkflowType WorkflowType { get; } Property Value DevopsWorkflowType

api/DecSm.Atom.Module.DevopsWorkflows.ID.summary: Interface IDevopsWorkflows Namespace DecSm.Atom.Module.DevopsWorkflows Assembly DecSm.Atom.Module.DevopsWorkflows.dll Provides integration with Azure DevOps Pipelines for DecSm.Atom builds. [PublicAPI] public interface IDevopsWorkflows : IJobRunsOn, IBuildAccessor Inherited Members IJobRunsOn.JobRunsOn IBuildAccessor.Services IBuildAccessor.Logger IBuildAccessor.AtomFileSystem IBuildAccessor.ProcessRunner IBuildAccessor.GetService<T>() IBuildAccessor.GetServices<T>() IBuildAccessor.GetParam<T>(Expression<Func<T>>, T, Func<string, T>) Remarks Implementing this interface configures the necessary services for generating Azure DevOps Pipeline YAML files, providing Azure DevOps-specific workflow variables, and adapting build paths and reporting when running within Azure DevOps Pipelines. Methods ConfigureBuilder(IHostApplicationBuilder) Configures the host builder to add Azure DevOps Pipelines workflow services. [UsedImplicitly] protected static void ConfigureBuilder(IHostApplicationBuilder builder) Parameters builder IHostApplicationBuilder The host application builder. Remarks This method registers DecSm.Atom.Module.DevopsWorkflows.Workflows.Devops.DevopsWorkflowFileWriter for generating workflow files and DecSm.Atom.Module.DevopsWorkflows.DevopsVariableProvider for Azure DevOps-specific workflow variables. When running inside Azure DevOps Pipelines, it also sets up DecSm.Atom.Module.DevopsWorkflows.DevopsSummaryOutcomeReportWriter for reporting and adjusts artifact and publish paths to Azure DevOps conventions.

api/DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.Cli.DotnetC.title: Class DotnetCliOptions | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.Cli.DotnetC.summary: Class DotnetCliOptions Namespace DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.Cli Assembly DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.dll Represents a set of options for configuring the execution of .NET CLI commands. [PublicAPI] public sealed record DotnetCliOptions Inheritance object DotnetCliOptions Remarks This record provides properties to control various aspects of how a .NET CLI process is run, including working directory, logging levels, error handling, environment variables, and output transformations. It is designed to be similar to ProcessRunOptions but specifically tailored for .NET CLI operations. Properties AllowFailedResult Gets or sets a value indicating whether the .NET CLI command execution should tolerate non-zero exit codes. public bool AllowFailedResult { get; init; } Property Value bool true if the command execution should return normally even when the exit code is non-zero; false if a StepFailedException should be thrown for non-zero exit codes. Defaults to false. Examples // Strict mode - throw on any failure (default) var strictOptions = new DotnetCliOptions(); // Tolerant mode - handle failures manually var tolerantOptions = new DotnetCliOptions { AllowFailedResult = true }; var result = await DotnetCli.RunAsync("test", tolerantOptions); if (result.ExitCode != 0) { // Handle the test failure case appropriately logger.LogWarning("dotnet test failed: {Error}", result.Error); } Remarks When set to false (the default), any non-zero exit code from the executed command will cause the IProcessRunner to throw a StepFailedException, which is appropriate for build automation scenarios where command failures should halt execution. When set to true, the command execution completes normally regardless of the exit code, allowing the caller to inspect the ExitCode and handle failures appropriately. This is useful for optional operations or when the exit code carries semantic meaning beyond success/failure. EnvironmentVariables Gets or sets the environment variables to be used by the .NET CLI process during execution. public Dictionary<string, string?> EnvironmentVariables { get; init; } Property Value Dictionary<string, string> A dictionary where keys represent the names of the environment variables and values represent their corresponding values. A value of null for any variable indicates that it should be removed from the environment. Remarks These environment variables are passed to the process when it is executed, potentially overriding inherited variables from the current environment. If not set, the process will inherit the current environment's variables by default. ErrorLogLevel Gets or sets the log level for standard error messages from the executed .NET CLI command. public LogLevel ErrorLogLevel { get; init; } Property Value LogLevel The Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LogLevel used when logging each line of standard error from the command. Defaults to Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LogLevel.Warning. Examples var options = new DotnetCliOptions { ErrorLogLevel = LogLevel.Error }; Remarks This property controls how error output from the .NET CLI command is logged. Warning level is the default as error output typically indicates issues that should be visible in production logs. For commands that use stderr for non-error information (like progress updates), consider using Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LogLevel.Debug or Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LogLevel.Information. InvocationLogLevel Gets or sets the log level for messages indicating the invocation of the .NET CLI command. public LogLevel InvocationLogLevel { get; init; } Property Value LogLevel The Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LogLevel used when logging the command start information, including the executable name, arguments, and working directory. Defaults to Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LogLevel.Information. Examples var options = new DotnetCliOptions { InvocationLogLevel = LogLevel.Debug }; Remarks This property controls the visibility of command invocation logging. Setting this to a higher level (e.g., Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LogLevel.Debug) reduces log verbosity in production scenarios, while lower levels (e.g., Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LogLevel.Trace) provide more detailed execution tracking. OutputLogLevel Gets or sets the log level for standard output messages from the executed .NET CLI command. public LogLevel OutputLogLevel { get; init; } Property Value LogLevel The Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LogLevel used when logging each line of standard output from the command. Defaults to Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LogLevel.Debug. Examples var options = new DotnetCliOptions { OutputLogLevel = LogLevel.Information }; Remarks This property controls how standard output from the .NET CLI command is logged. Debug level is the default as it provides detailed execution information without overwhelming production logs. For commands that produce important output that should always be visible, consider using Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LogLevel.Information or higher. TransformError An optional per-line transformation applied to the standard error (stderr) of the executed .NET CLI command. public Func<string, string?>? TransformError { get; init; } Property Value Func<string, string> A delegate that receives the raw line of text read from stderr (without a trailing newline) and returns the transformed text to be logged and captured. Returning null suppresses the line completely (it will neither be logged nor stored in Error). If not set, the original text is used as-is. Examples var options = new DotnetCliOptions { // Strip ANSI escape sequences from dotnet run stderr TransformError = line => System.Text.RegularExpressions.Regex.Replace(line, "\u001B\\[[0-9;]*[A-Za-z]", string.Empty) }; Remarks Some .NET CLI commands might write progress or informational messages to stderr. Use this transformation to downsample, redact, or normalize such messages. As with TransformOutput, keep the delegate efficient to avoid affecting command throughput. See Also TransformOutput TransformOutput An optional per-line transformation applied to the standard output (stdout) of the executed .NET CLI command. public Func<string, string?>? TransformOutput { get; init; } Property Value Func<string, string> A delegate that receives the raw line of text read from stdout (without a trailing newline) and returns the transformed text to be logged and captured. Returning null suppresses the line completely (it will neither be logged nor stored in Output). If not set, the original text is used as-is. Examples var options = new DotnetCliOptions { // Hide lines that start with "info :" from dotnet build output TransformOutput = line => line.StartsWith("info :", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) ? null : line }; Remarks The transformation is invoked once for each line of stdout as it is produced by the command. Use this to filter noisy lines, redact sensitive data (like tokens), or normalize output (e.g., remove ANSI color codes). Keep the transformation fast and side-effect free; it may be called frequently for verbose commands. See Also TransformError WorkingDirectory Gets or sets the working directory for the .NET CLI command execution. public string? WorkingDirectory { get; init; } Property Value string The path to the directory where the command should be executed, or null to use the current application's working directory. Relative paths are resolved relative to the current working directory. Examples var options = new DotnetCliOptions { WorkingDirectory = "./src/MyProject" }; Remarks When specified, this directory becomes the current working directory for the child process, affecting relative path resolution and file operations within the executed command. The directory must exist when the process is started, or the execution will fail.

NOTE: The command name will be overridden by the appropriate dotnet command cancellationToken CancellationToken Cancellation token Returns Task<ProcessRunResult> WorkloadSearchVersion(IEnumerable<string>, WorkloadSearchVersionOptions?, ProcessRunOptions?, CancellationToken) 'dotnet workload search version' has three functions depending on its argument: 1. If no argument is specified, it outputs a list of the latest released workload versions from this feature band. Takes the --take option to specify how many to provide and --format to alter the format. Example: dotnet workload search version --take 2 --format json [{"workloadVersion":"9.0.201"},{"workloadVersion":"9.0.200.1"}] 2. If a workload version is provided as an argument, it outputs a table of various workloads and their versions for the specified workload version. Takes the --format option to alter the output format. Example: dotnet workload search version 9.0.201 Workload manifest ID Manifest feature band Manifest Version ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ microsoft.net.workload.emscripten.current 9.0.100-rc.1 9.0.0-rc.1.24430.3 microsoft.net.workload.emscripten.net6 9.0.100-rc.1 9.0.0-rc.1.24430.3 microsoft.net.workload.emscripten.net7 9.0.100-rc.1 9.0.0-rc.1.24430.3 microsoft.net.workload.emscripten.net8 9.0.100-rc.1 9.0.0-rc.1.24430.3 microsoft.net.sdk.android 9.0.100-rc.1 35.0.0-rc.1.80 microsoft.net.sdk.ios 9.0.100-rc.1 17.5.9270-net9-rc1 microsoft.net.sdk.maccatalyst 9.0.100-rc.1 17.5.9270-net9-rc1 microsoft.net.sdk.macos 9.0.100-rc.1 14.5.9270-net9-rc1 microsoft.net.sdk.maui 9.0.100-rc.1 9.0.0-rc.1.24453.9 microsoft.net.sdk.tvos 9.0.100-rc.1 17.5.9270-net9-rc1 microsoft.net.workload.mono.toolchain.current 9.0.100-rc.1 9.0.0-rc.1.24431.7 microsoft.net.workload.mono.toolchain.net6 9.0.100-rc.1 9.0.0-rc.1.24431.7 microsoft.net.workload.mono.toolchain.net7 9.0.100-rc.1 9.0.0-rc.1.24431.7 microsoft.net.workload.mono.toolchain.net8 9.0.100-rc.1 9.0.0-rc.1.24431.7 3. If one or more workloads are provided along with their versions (by joining them with the '@' character), it outputs workload versions that match the provided versions. Takes the --take option to specify how many to provide and --format to alter the format. Example: dotnet workload search version maui@9.0.0-rc.1.24453.9 ios@17.5.9270-net9-rc1 9.0.201 Task<ProcessRunResult> WorkloadSearchVersion(IEnumerable<string> workloadVersion, WorkloadSearchVersionOptions? options = null, ProcessRunOptions? processRunOptions = null, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters workloadVersion IEnumerable<string> Output workload manifest versions associated with the provided workload version. options WorkloadSearchVersionOptions Options processRunOptions ProcessRunOptions Process run options. api/DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.Cli.TestOpt.title: Class TestOptions | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.Cli.TestOpt.summary: Class TestOptions Namespace DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.Cli Assembly DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.dll [PublicAPI] public sealed record TestOptions Inheritance object TestOptions Properties Arch The target architecture. public string? Arch { get; init; } Property Value string ArtifactsPath The artifacts path. All output from the project, including build, publish, and pack output, will go in subfolders under the specified path. public string? ArtifactsPath { get; init; } Property Value string Blame Runs the tests in blame mode. This option is helpful in isolating problematic tests that cause the test host to crash or hang, but it does not create a memory dump by default. When a crash is detected, it creates an sequence file in TestResults/guid/guid_Sequence.xml that captures the order of tests that were run before the crash. Based on the additional settings, hang dump or crash dump can also be collected. Example: Timeout the test run when test takes more than the default timeout of 1 hour, and collect crash dump when the test host exits unexpectedly. (Crash dumps require additional setup, see below.) dotnet test --blame-hang --blame-crash Example: Timeout the test run when a test takes more than 20 minutes and collect hang dump. dotnet test --blame-hang-timeout 20min public bool? Blame { get; init; } Property Value bool? BlameCrash Runs the tests in blame mode and collects a crash dump when the test host exits unexpectedly. This option depends on the version of .NET used, the type of error, and the operating system. For exceptions in managed code, a dump will be automatically collected on .NET 5.0 and later versions. It will generate a dump for testhost or any child process that also ran on .NET 5.0 and crashed. Crashes in native code will not generate a dump. This option works on Windows, macOS, and Linux. Crash dumps in native code, or when targetting .NET Framework, or .NET Core 3.1 and earlier versions, can only be collected on Windows, by using Procdump. A directory that contains procdump.exe and procdump64.exe must be in the PATH or PROCDUMP_PATH environment variable. The tools can be downloaded here: https://docs.microsoft.com/sysinternals/downloads/procdump To collect a crash dump from a native application running on .NET 5.0 or later, the usage of Procdump can be forced by setting the VSTEST_DUMP_FORCEPROCDUMP environment variable to 1. Implies --blame. public bool? BlameCrash { get; init; } Property Value bool? BlameCrashCollectAlways Enables collecting crash dump on expected as well as unexpected testhost exit. public bool? BlameCrashCollectAlways { get; init; } Property Value bool? BlameCrashDumpType The type of crash dump to be collected. Supported values are full (default) and mini. Implies --blame-crash. public string? BlameCrashDumpType { get; init; } Property Value string BlameHang Run the tests in blame mode and enables collecting hang dump when test exceeds the given timeout. public bool? BlameHang { get; init; } Property Value bool? BlameHangDumpType The type of crash dump to be collected. The supported values are full (default), mini, and none. When 'none' is used then test host is terminated on timeout, but no dump is collected. Implies --blame-hang. public string? BlameHangDumpType { get; init; } Property Value string BlameHangTimeout Per-test timeout, after which hang dump is triggered and the testhost process is terminated. Default is 1h. The timeout value is specified in the following format: 1.5h / 90m / 5400s / 5400000ms. When no unit is used (e.g. 5400000), the value is assumed to be in milliseconds. When used together with data driven tests, the timeout behavior depends on the test adapter used. For xUnit, NUnit and MSTest 2.2.4+ the timeout is renewed after every test case, For MSTest before 2.2.4, the timeout is used for all testcases. public string? BlameHangTimeout { get; init; } Property Value string Collect The friendly name of the data collector to use for the test run. More info here: https://aka.ms/vstest-collect public IEnumerable<string>? Collect { get; init; } Property Value IEnumerable<string> Configuration The configuration to use for running tests. api/DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.Cli.Verbosi.summary: Enum VerbosityOptions Namespace DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.Cli Assembly DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.dll Specifies the verbosity level for .NET CLI commands. [PublicAPI] public enum VerbosityOptions Fields Detailed = 3 Shows detailed output, including more diagnostic information. Diagnostic = 4 Shows diagnostic output, which is the most verbose level, including all internal logging. Minimal = 1 Shows minimal output, typically just progress and summary information. Normal = 2 Shows normal output, which is the default for most commands. Quiet = 0 Suppresses all output except for errors and warnings.

api/DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.Helpers.IDo.title: Interface IDotnetPackHelper | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.Helpers.IDo.summary:
Interface IDotnetPackHelper Namespace DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.Helpers Assembly DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.dll Provides helper methods for packing .NET projects into NuGet packages and staging them for publishing. [PublicAPI] public interface IDotnetPackHelper : IDotnetCliHelper, IBuildInfo, IBuildAccessor Inherited Members IDotnetCliHelper.DotnetCli IDotnetCliHelper.ConfigureBuilder(IHostApplicationBuilder) IBuildInfo.BuildName IBuildInfo.BuildId IBuildInfo.BuildVersion IBuildInfo.BuildTimestamp IBuildInfo.BuildSlice IBuildAccessor.Services IBuildAccessor.Logger IBuildAccessor.AtomFileSystem IBuildAccessor.ProcessRunner IBuildAccessor.GetService() IBuildAccessor.GetServices() IBuildAccessor.GetParam(Expression>, T, Func) Remarks This interface extends IDotnetCliHelper to utilize the .NET CLI for packing operations and IBuildInfo to leverage build version information. Methods DotnetPackAndStage(RootedPath, DotnetPackAndStageOptions?, CancellationToken) Packs a .NET project specified by its path and stages the resulting NuGet package. [PublicAPI] Task DotnetPackAndStage(RootedPath projectPath, DotnetPackAndStageOptions? options = null, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters projectPath RootedPath The full path to the project file (e.g., "src/MyProject/MyProject.csproj"). options DotnetPackAndStageOptions Optional. Configuration options for the packing and staging process. cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token to observe while waiting for the task to complete. Returns Task A System.Threading.Tasks.Task representing the asynchronous operation. Remarks This method performs the following steps:
  1. Determines the output directory for the NuGet package.
  2. Cleans up existing package directories if specified.
  3. Applies version transformations to project files if is true.
  4. Executes the `dotnet pack` command using .
  5. Locates the generated `.nupkg` file.
  6. Moves the `.nupkg` file to the Atom publish directory for the project.
The BuildVersion is used for naming the NuGet package and for version transformation. Exceptions StepFailedException Thrown if the packed NuGet package is not found after the operation. DotnetPackAndStage(string, DotnetPackAndStageOptions?, CancellationToken) Packs a .NET project by its name and stages the resulting NuGet package. [PublicAPI] Task DotnetPackAndStage(string projectName, DotnetPackAndStageOptions? options = null, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters projectName string The name of the project to pack (e.g., "MyProject.Core"). options DotnetPackAndStageOptions Optional. Configuration options for the packing and staging process. cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token to observe while waiting for the task to complete. Returns Task A System.Threading.Tasks.Task representing the asynchronous operation. Remarks This method first locates the project file based on its name, then calls DotnetPackAndStage(RootedPath, DotnetPackAndStageOptions?, CancellationToken) with the resolved path. Exceptions StepFailedException Thrown if the project file cannot be located.

api/DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.Helpers.IDo.title: Interface IDotnetPublishHelper | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.Helpers.IDo.summary:
Interface IDotnetPublishHelper Namespace DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.Helpers Assembly DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.dll Provides helper methods for publishing .NET projects and staging their output. [PublicAPI] public interface IDotnetPublishHelper : IDotnetCliHelper, IBuildInfo, IBuildAccessor Inherited Members IDotnetCliHelper.DotnetCli IDotnetCliHelper.ConfigureBuilder(IHostApplicationBuilder) IBuildInfo.BuildName IBuildInfo.BuildId IBuildInfo.BuildVersion IBuildInfo.BuildTimestamp IBuildInfo.BuildSlice IBuildAccessor.Services IBuildAccessor.Logger IBuildAccessor.AtomFileSystem IBuildAccessor.ProcessRunner IBuildAccessor.GetService() IBuildAccessor.GetServices() IBuildAccessor.GetParam(Expression>, T, Func) Remarks This interface extends IDotnetCliHelper to utilize the .NET CLI for publishing operations and IBuildInfo to leverage build version information. Methods DotnetPublishAndStage(RootedPath, DotnetPublishAndStageOptions?, CancellationToken) Publishes a .NET project specified by its path and stages the output. [PublicAPI] Task DotnetPublishAndStage(RootedPath projectPath, DotnetPublishAndStageOptions? options = null, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters projectPath RootedPath The full path to the project file (e.g., "src/MyWebApp/MyWebApp.csproj"). options DotnetPublishAndStageOptions Optional. Configuration options for the publishing and staging process. cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token to observe while waiting for the task to complete. Returns Task A System.Threading.Tasks.Task representing the asynchronous operation. Remarks This method performs the following steps:
  1. Determines the output directory for the published application.
  2. Cleans up existing publish directories if specified.
  3. Applies version transformations to project files if is true.
  4. Executes the `dotnet publish` command using .
  5. Moves the published output to the Atom publish directory for the project.
The BuildVersion is used for version transformation. DotnetPublishAndStage(string, DotnetPublishAndStageOptions?, CancellationToken) Publishes a .NET project by its name and stages the output. [PublicAPI] Task DotnetPublishAndStage(string projectName, DotnetPublishAndStageOptions? options = null, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters projectName string The name of the project to publish (e.g., "MyWebApp"). options DotnetPublishAndStageOptions Optional. Configuration options for the publishing and staging process. cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token to observe while waiting for the task to complete. Interface IDotnetTestHelper Namespace DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.Helpers Assembly DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.dll Provides helper methods for running .NET tests and staging their results and code coverage reports. [PublicAPI] public interface IDotnetTestHelper : IDotnetCliHelper, IBuildInfo, IDotnetToolInstallHelper, IReportsHelper, IBuildAccessor Inherited Members IDotnetCliHelper.DotnetCli IDotnetCliHelper.ConfigureBuilder(IHostApplicationBuilder) IBuildInfo.BuildName IBuildInfo.BuildId IBuildInfo.BuildVersion IBuildInfo.BuildTimestamp IBuildInfo.BuildSlice IDotnetToolInstallHelper.InstallTool(string, string, bool, bool) IDotnetToolInstallHelper.InstallToolAsync(string, string, bool, bool, CancellationToken) IReportsHelper.AddReportData(IReportData) IBuildAccessor.Services IBuildAccessor.Logger IBuildAccessor.AtomFileSystem IBuildAccessor.ProcessRunner IBuildAccessor.GetService() IBuildAccessor.GetServices() IBuildAccessor.GetParam(Expression>, T, Func) Remarks This interface extends IDotnetCliHelper for executing dotnet test commands, IBuildInfo for build version information, IDotnetToolInstallHelper for installing necessary tools, and IReportsHelper for generating build reports. Methods DotnetTestAndStage(RootedPath, DotnetTestAndStageOptions?, CancellationToken) Runs tests for a .NET project specified by its path, then stages the test results and optionally code coverage reports. [PublicAPI] Task DotnetTestAndStage(RootedPath projectPath, DotnetTestAndStageOptions? options = null, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters projectPath RootedPath The full path to the project file (e.g., "tests/MyProject.Tests/MyProject.Tests.csproj"). options DotnetTestAndStageOptions Optional. Configuration options for the testing and staging process. cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token to observe while waiting for the task to complete. Returns Task A System.Threading.Tasks.Task that returns the exit code of the dotnet test command. Remarks This method performs the following steps:
  1. Configures `dotnet test` options, including TRX and HTML loggers, and optionally code coverage.
  2. Cleans up existing test result directories.
  3. Applies version transformations to project files if is true.
  4. Executes the `dotnet test` command using .
  5. Copies the generated HTML test report to the Atom publish directory.
  6. Generates a detailed test report using .
  7. If is true, installs `dotnet-reportgenerator-globaltool` and generates code coverage reports.
  8. Generates a code coverage report using .
The BuildVersion is used for version transformation. DotnetTestAndStage(string, DotnetTestAndStageOptions?, CancellationToken) Runs tests for a .NET project by its name, then stages the test results and optionally code coverage reports. [PublicAPI] Task DotnetTestAndStage(string projectName, DotnetTestAndStageOptions? options = null, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters projectName string The name of the project to test (e.g., "MyProject.Tests"). options DotnetTestAndStageOptions Optional. Configuration options for the testing and staging process. cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token to observe while waiting for the task to complete. Returns Task A System.Threading.Tasks.Task that returns the exit code of the dotnet test command. Remarks This method first locates the project file based on its name, then calls DotnetTestAndStage(RootedPath, DotnetTestAndStageOptions?, CancellationToken) with the resolved path. Exceptions StepFailedException Thrown if the project file cannot be located. GenerateCoverageReport(string, string?, string?, string, bool) Generates a code coverage report from a JSON summary file and adds it to the build report. [PublicAPI] void GenerateCoverageReport(string projectName, string? configuration, string? framework, string coverageJsonFile, bool includeTitle = true) Parameters projectName string The name of the project for which coverage was generated. configuration string The build configuration (e.g., "Release", "Debug"). framework string The target framework (e.g., "net8.0"). coverageJsonFile string The path to the JSON code coverage summary file. includeTitle bool Whether to include a title in the generated report data. GenerateTestReport(string, string?, string?, string, bool) Generates a test report from a TRX file and adds it to the build report. [UnconditionalSuppressMessage("Trimming", "IL2026:RequiresUnreferencedCode", Justification = "Deserialized type `TestRun` is manually preserved via DynamicallyAccessedMembers.")] [PublicAPI] void GenerateTestReport(string projectName, string? configuration, string? framework, string trxFile, bool includeTitle = true) Parameters projectName string The name of the project that was tested. configuration string The build configuration (e.g., "Release", "Debug"). framework string The target framework (e.g., "net8.0"). trxFile string The path to the TRX test results file. includeTitle bool Whether to include a title in the generated report data.

api/DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.Helpers.IDo.summary: Interface IDotnetToolInstallHelper Namespace DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.Helpers Assembly DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.dll Provides helper methods for installing and managing .NET CLI tools. [PublicAPI] public interface IDotnetToolInstallHelper : IBuildAccessor Inherited Members IBuildAccessor.Services IBuildAccessor.Logger IBuildAccessor.AtomFileSystem IBuildAccessor.ProcessRunner IBuildAccessor.GetService<T>() IBuildAccessor.GetServices<T>() IBuildAccessor.GetParam<T>(Expression<Func<T>>, T, Func<string, T>) Remarks This interface extends IBuildAccessor to gain access to core build services like IProcessRunner and IAtomFileSystem. Methods InstallTool(string, string?, bool, bool) Installs or updates a .NET CLI tool. [PublicAPI] void InstallTool(string toolName, string? version = null, bool global = true, bool forceReinstall = false) Parameters toolName string The name of the tool to install (e.g., "dotnet-format"). version string Optional. The specific version of the tool to install. If not specified, the latest stable version is used. global bool If true, the tool is installed globally. If false, it's installed locally to the current directory. forceReinstall bool If true, the tool will be reinstalled even if already present. Remarks This method first checks if the tool is already installed (unless forceReinstall is true or running in headless mode). If not installed or reinstallation is forced, it executes `dotnet tool update`. If installing locally (global is false) and a tool manifest (`.config/dotnet-tools.json`) does not exist, it will be created. InstallToolAsync(string, string?, bool, bool, CancellationToken) Asynchronously installs or updates a .NET CLI tool. [PublicAPI] Task InstallToolAsync(string toolName, string? version = null, bool global = true, bool forceReinstall = false, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters toolName string The name of the tool to install (e.g., "dotnet-format"). version string Optional. The specific version of the tool to install. If not specified, the latest stable version is used. global bool If true, the tool is installed globally. If false, it's installed locally to the current directory. forceReinstall bool If true, the tool will be reinstalled even if already present. cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token to observe while waiting for the task to complete. Returns Task A System.Threading.Tasks.Task representing the asynchronous operation. Remarks This method first checks if the tool is already installed (unless forceReinstall is true or running in headless mode). If not installed or reinstallation is forced, it executes `dotnet tool update`. If installing locally (global is false) and a tool manifest (`.config/dotnet-tools.json`) does not exist, it will be created.

api/DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.Helpers.INu.title: Interface INugetHelper | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.Helpers.INu.summary: Interface INugetHelper Namespace DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.Helpers Assembly DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.dll Provides helper methods for interacting with NuGet, such as pushing packages and managing NuGet configuration. [PublicAPI] public interface INugetHelper : IBuildInfo, IBuildAccessor Inherited Members IBuildInfo.BuildName IBuildInfo.BuildId IBuildInfo.BuildVersion IBuildInfo.BuildTimestamp IBuildInfo.BuildSlice IBuildAccessor.Services IBuildAccessor.Logger IBuildAccessor.AtomFileSystem IBuildAccessor.ProcessRunner IBuildAccessor.GetService<T>() IBuildAccessor.GetServices<T>() IBuildAccessor.GetParam<T>(Expression<Func<T>>, T, Func<string, T>) Remarks This interface extends IBuildInfo to leverage build version information and provides functionality for common NuGet operations within a DecSm.Atom build. Properties NugetConfigPath Gets the path to the default NuGet configuration file (NuGet.Config) for the current operating system. RootedPath NugetConfigPath { get; } Property Value RootedPath Remarks This property determines the standard location where NuGet configuration files are expected. On Windows, it's typically %APPDATA%\NuGet\NuGet.Config. On Linux/macOS, it's typically $HOME/.nuget/NuGet.Config. NugetDryRun Gets a value indicating whether NuGet write operations (like pushing packages) should be performed as a dry run. [ParamDefinition("nuget-dry-run", "Whether to perform a dry run of nuget write operations.", ParamSource.All, null)] bool NugetDryRun { get; } Property Value bool Remarks When true, NuGet operations that modify remote feeds will be simulated and logged, but no actual changes will be made. This is useful for testing build configurations. Methods CreateNugetConfigOverwriteScope(NugetFeed[], bool, CancellationToken) Creates a TransformFileScope that temporarily overwrites the default NuGet.Config file with a configuration that includes the specified NuGet feeds and their credentials. [PublicAPI] Task CreateNugetConfigOverwriteScope(NugetFeed[] feeds, bool skipIfExists = false, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters feeds NugetFeed[] An array of NugetFeed objects to include in the configuration. skipIfExists bool If true, and all specified feeds are already present in the existing NuGet.Config, no overwrite will occur, and an empty scope will be returned. cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token to observe while waiting for the task to complete. Returns Task A System.Threading.Tasks.Task<TResult> that returns a TransformFileScope instance. Disposing this scope will restore the original NuGet.Config file. Remarks This method generates a NuGet.Config XML structure including <packageSources> and < packageSourceCredentials> based on the provided feed information. Passwords are included as clear text if specified via PlainTextPassword. CreateNugetConfigOverwriteScope(string, CancellationToken) Creates a TransformFileScope that temporarily overwrites the default NuGet.Config file with the provided content. [PublicAPI] Task<TransformFileScope> CreateNugetConfigOverwriteScope(string contents, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters contents string The new content for the NuGet.Config file. cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token to observe while waiting for the task to complete. Returns Task<TransformFileScope> A System.Threading.Tasks.Task<TResult> that returns a TransformFileScope instance. Disposing this scope will restore the original NuGet.Config file. IsNugetPackageVersionPublished(string, string, string, string?, CancellationToken) Checks if a specific version of a NuGet package is already published to a given feed. [PublicAPI] Task<bool> IsNugetPackageVersionPublished(string projectName, string version, string feedUrl, string? feedKey = null, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters projectName string The name of the NuGet package (e.g., "MyProject.Core"). version string The semantic version of the package (e.g., "1.0.0"). feedUrl string The URL of the NuGet feed (e.g., "https://api.nuget.org/v3/index.json"). feedKey string Optional API key for authenticated feeds. cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token to observe while waiting for the task to complete. Returns Task<bool> A System.Threading.Tasks.Task<TResult> that returns true if the package version is published, otherwise false . Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown if the NuGet feed's registration base URL cannot be found or if the status check fails unexpectedly. PushPackageToNuget(RootedPath, string, string, RootedPath?, CancellationToken) Pushes a specific NuGet package file to a NuGet feed. [PublicAPI] Task PushPackageToNuget(RootedPath packagePath, string feed, string apiKey, RootedPath? configFile = null, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters packagePath RootedPath The full path to the .nupkg file to push. feed string The NuGet feed URL to push the package to. apiKey string The API key for authenticating with the NuGet feed. configFile RootedPath Optional path to a NuGet configuration file to use instead of the default one. cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token to observe while waiting for the task to complete. Returns Task A System.Threading.Tasks.Task representing the asynchronous operation. Remarks If NugetDryRun is true, the push operation will be simulated. PushProject(string, string, string, RootedPath?, CancellationToken) Pushes a NuGet package for a specific project to a NuGet feed. [PublicAPI] Task PushProject(string projectName, string feed, string apiKey, RootedPath? configFile = null, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters projectName string The name of the project whose package is to be pushed. feed string The NuGet feed URL to push the package to. apiKey string The API key for authenticating with the NuGet feed. configFile RootedPath Optional path to a NuGet configuration file to use instead of the default one. cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token to observe while waiting for the task to complete. Returns Task A System.Threading.Tasks.Task representing the asynchronous operation. Remarks This method expects the project to have been built and its NuGet package (`.nupkg`) to be available in the Atom artifacts directory, named according to the project name and the BuildVersion. It requires the `dotnet` CLI to be installed and available in the system's PATH. If NugetDryRun is true, the push operation will be simulated.

api/DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.Util.Dotnet.title: Class DotnetFileUtil | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.Util.Dotnet.summary: Class DotnetFileUtil Namespace DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.Util Assembly DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.dll [PublicAPI] public static class DotnetFileUtil Inheritance object DotnetFileUtil Methods GetProjectFilePathByName(IAtomFileSystem, string) Gets the full path to a project file by its name. public static RootedPath? GetProjectFilePathByName(IAtomFileSystem atomFileSystem, string projectName) Parameters atomFileSystem IAtomFileSystem The file system to use. projectName string The name of the project. Returns RootedPath The full path to the project file. Remarks Searches for a file matching the pattern {projectName}.*proj in all subdirectories of the root directory. If multiple files match, the one with the shortest path ancestry (fewest directory levels) is returned. Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown if the project file does not exist. GetPropertyFilesForProject(RootedPath, RootedPath) Finds the Directory.Build.props files that apply to the given project file. public static IEnumerable<RootedPath> GetPropertyFilesForProject(RootedPath projectFilePath, RootedPath rootPath) Parameters projectFilePath RootedPath The full path to the project file. rootPath RootedPath The root path for the operation. Directories above this path will not be searched. Returns IEnumerable<RootedPath> The project file as well as any Directory.Build.props files that apply to it.

api/DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.Util.MsBuil.title: Class MsBuildUtil | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.Util.MsBuil.summary: Class MsBuildUtil Namespace DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.Util Assembly DecSm.Atom.Module.Dotnet.dll Provides utility methods for working with MSBuild project files, specifically for managing version information. [PublicAPI] public static class MsBuildUtil Inheritance object MsBuildUtil Remarks This utility class offers functionality to manipulate MSBuild project files by updating version-related properties. It ensures consistent version management across different version properties in MSBuild projects. Methods SetVersionInfo(string, SemVer) Updates an MSBuild project file XML with version information from a semantic version. public static string SetVersionInfo(string file, SemVer version) Parameters file string The MSBuild project file content as an XML string. version SemVer The semantic version to apply to the project file. Returns string The updated MSBuild project file content as an XML string with version properties set. Examples var projectXml = File.ReadAllText("MyProject.csproj"); var version = new SemVer("1.2.3-beta.4"); var updatedXml = MsBuildUtil.SetVersionInfo(projectXml, version); File.WriteAllText("MyProject.csproj", updatedXml); Remarks This method performs the following operations: Removes all existing version-related properties from PropertyGroup elements Creates a new PropertyGroup with standardized version properties Sets Version to the semantic version prefix Sets PackageVersion to the full semantic version Sets AssemblyVersion to the prefix with ".0" appended Sets FileVersion to the prefix with build number from pre-release Sets InformationalVersion to the full semantic version The method removes duplicate version properties to ensure clean project files and consistent versioning. Exceptions XmlException Thrown when the input file content is not valid XML.
