api/DecSm.Atom.Module.GithubWorkflows.Ex.title: Class WorkflowExpressionExtensions.Expressions | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Module.GithubWorkflows.Ex.summary: Class WorkflowExpressionExtensions.Expressions Namespace DecSm.Atom.Module.GithubWorkflows.Extensions Assembly DecSm.Atom.Module.GithubWorkflows.dll [PublicAPI] public sealed class WorkflowExpressionExtensions.Expressions Inheritance object WorkflowExpressionExtensions.Expressions Properties Env The env context contains variables that have been set in a workflow, job, or step. It does not contain variables inherited by the runner process. public RawExpression Env { get; } Property Value RawExpression Github The github context contains information about the workflow run and the event that triggered the run. You can read most of the github context data in environment variables. For more information about environment variables, see Store information in variables. public RawExpression Github { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubAction The name of the action currently running, or the id of a step. GitHub removes special characters, and uses the name __run when the current step runs a script without an id. If you use the same action more than once in the same job, the name will include a suffix with the sequence number with underscore before it. For example, the first script you run will have the name __run, and the second script will be named __run_2. Similarly, the second invocation of actions/checkout will be actionscheckout2. public RawExpression GithubAction { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubActionPath The path where an action is located. This property is only supported in composite actions. You can use this path to access files located in the same repository as the action, for example by changing directories to the path (using the corresponding environment variable): cd "$GITHUB_ACTION_PATH". For more information on environment variables, see Secure use reference . public RawExpression GithubActionPath { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubActionRef For a step executing an action, this is the ref of the action being executed. For example, v2. public RawExpression GithubActionRef { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubActionRepository For a step executing an action, this is the owner and repository name of the action. For example, actions/checkout. public RawExpression GithubActionRepository { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubActionStatus For a composite action, the current result of the composite action. public RawExpression GithubActionStatus { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubActor The username of the user that triggered the initial workflow run. If the workflow run is a re-run, this value may differ from github.triggering_actor. Any workflow re-runs will use the privileges of github.actor, even if the actor initiating the re-run (github.triggering_actor) has different privileges. public RawExpression GithubActor { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubActorId The account ID of the person or app that triggered the initial workflow run. For example, 1234567. Note that this is different from the actor username. public RawExpression GithubActorId { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubApiUrl The URL of the GitHub REST API. public RawExpression GithubApiUrl { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubBaseRef The base_ref or target branch of the pull request in a workflow run. This property is only available when the event that triggers a workflow run is either pull_request or pull_request_target. public RawExpression GithubBaseRef { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubEnv Path on the runner to the file that sets environment variables from workflow commands. This file is unique to the current step and is a different file for each step in a job. public RawExpression GithubEnv { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubEvent The full event webhook payload. You can access individual properties of the event using this context. This object is identical to the webhook payload of the event that triggered the workflow run, and is different for each event. public RawExpression GithubEvent { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubEventName The name of the event that triggered the workflow run. public RawExpression GithubEventName { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubEventPath The path to the file on the runner that contains the full event webhook payload. public RawExpression GithubEventPath { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubGraphqlUrl The URL of the GitHub GraphQL API. public RawExpression GithubGraphqlUrl { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubHeadRef The head_ref or source branch of the pull request in a workflow run. This property is only available when the event that triggers a workflow run is either pull_request or pull_request_target. public RawExpression GithubHeadRef { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubJob The job_id of the current job. Note: This context property is set by the Actions runner, and is only available within the execution steps of a job. Otherwise, the value of this property will be null. public RawExpression GithubJob { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubPath Path on the runner to the file that sets system PATH variables from workflow commands. This file is unique to the current step and is a different file for each step in a job. public RawExpression GithubPath { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubRef The fully-formed ref of the branch or tag that triggered the workflow run. For branches the format is refs/heads/<branch_name>. For pull requests events except pull_request_target that were not merged, it is refs/pull/<pr_number>/merge. For tags it is refs/tags/<tag_name>. For example, refs/heads/feature-branch-1. public RawExpression GithubRef { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubRefName The short ref name of the branch or tag that triggered the workflow run. This value matches the branch or tag name shown on GitHub. For example, feature-branch-1. For pull requests that were not merged, the format is <pr_number>/merge. public RawExpression GithubRefName { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubRefProtected true if branch protections or rulesets are configured for the ref that triggered the workflow run. public RawExpression GithubRefProtected { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubRefType The type of ref that triggered the workflow run. Valid values are branch or tag. public RawExpression GithubRefType { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubRepository The owner and repository name. For example, octocat/Hello-World. public RawExpression GithubRepository { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubRepositoryId The ID of the repository. For example, 123456789. Note that this is different from the repository name. public RawExpression GithubRepositoryId { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubRepositoryOwner The repository owner's username. For example, octocat. public RawExpression GithubRepositoryOwner { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubRepositoryOwnerId The repository owner's account ID. For example, 1234567. Note that this is different from the owner's name. public RawExpression GithubRepositoryOwnerId { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubRepositoryUrl The Git URL to the repository. For example, git://github.com/octocat/hello-world.git. public RawExpression GithubRepositoryUrl { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubRetentionDays The number of days that workflow run logs and artifacts are kept. public RawExpression GithubRetentionDays { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubRunAttempt A unique number for each attempt of a particular workflow run in a repository. This number begins at 1 for the workflow run's first attempt, and increments with each re-run. public RawExpression GithubRunAttempt { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubRunId A unique number for each workflow run within a repository. This number does not change if you re-run the workflow run. public RawExpression GithubRunId { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubRunNumber A unique number for each run of a particular workflow in a repository. This number begins at 1 for the workflow's first run, and increments with each new run. This number does not change if you re-run the workflow run. public RawExpression GithubRunNumber { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubSecretSource The source of a secret used in a workflow. Possible values are None, Actions, Codespaces, or Dependabot. public RawExpression GithubSecretSource { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubServerUrl The URL of the GitHub server. For example: https://github.com. public RawExpression GithubServerUrl { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubSha The commit SHA that triggered the workflow. The value of this commit SHA depends on the event that triggered the workflow. For example, ffac537e6cbbf934b08745a378932722df287a53. public RawExpression GithubSha { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubToken A token to authenticate on behalf of the GitHub App installed on your repository. This is functionally equivalent to the GITHUB_TOKEN secret. Note: This context property is set by the Actions runner, and is only available within the execution steps of a job. Otherwise, the value of this property will be null. public RawExpression GithubToken { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubTriggeringActor The username of the user that initiated the workflow run. If the workflow run is a re-run, this value may differ from github.actor. Any workflow re-runs will use the privileges of github.actor, even if the actor initiating the re-run (github.triggering_actor) has different privileges. public RawExpression GithubTriggeringActor { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubWorkflow The name of the workflow. If the workflow file doesn't specify a name, the value of this property is the full path of the workflow file in the repository. public RawExpression GithubWorkflow { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubWorkflowRef The ref path to the workflow. For example, octocat/hello-world/.github/workflows/my-workflow.yml@refs/heads/my_branch. public RawExpression GithubWorkflowRef { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubWorkflowSha The commit SHA for the workflow file. public RawExpression GithubWorkflowSha { get; } Property Value RawExpression GithubWorkspace The default working directory on the runner for steps, and the default location of your repository when using the checkout action. public RawExpression GithubWorkspace { get; } Property Value RawExpression Inputs This context is only available in a reusable workflow or in a workflow triggered by the workflow_dispatch event. You can access this context from any job or step in a workflow. This object contains the properties listed below. public RawExpression Inputs { get; } Property Value RawExpression Job This context changes for each job in a workflow run. You can access this context from any step in a job. This object contains all the properties listed below. public RawExpression Job { get; } Property Value RawExpression JobCheckRunId The check run ID of the current job. public RawExpression JobCheckRunId { get; } Property Value RawExpression JobContainer Information about the job's container. public RawExpression JobContainer { get; } Property Value RawExpression JobContainerId The ID of the container. public RawExpression JobContainerId { get; } Property Value RawExpression JobContainerNetwork The ID of the container network. The runner creates the network used by all containers in a job. public RawExpression JobContainerNetwork { get; } Property Value RawExpression JobServices The service containers created for a job. public RawExpression JobServices { get; } Property Value RawExpression JobStatus The current status of the job. Possible values are success, failure, or cancelled. public RawExpression JobStatus { get; } Property Value RawExpression Jobs This is only available in reusable workflows, and can only be used to set outputs for a reusable workflow. This object contains all the properties listed below. public RawExpression Jobs { get; } Property Value RawExpression Matrix This context is only available for jobs in a matrix, and changes for each job in a workflow run. You can access this context from any job or step in a workflow. This object contains the properties listed below. public RawExpression Matrix { get; } Property Value RawExpression Needs This context is only populated for workflow runs that have dependent jobs, and changes for each job in a workflow run. You can access this context from any job or step in a workflow. This object contains all the properties listed below. public RawExpression Needs { get; } Property Value RawExpression Runner This context changes for each job in a workflow run. This object contains all the properties listed below. public RawExpression Runner { get; } Property Value RawExpression RunnerArch The architecture of the runner executing the job. Possible values are X86, X64, ARM, or ARM64. public RawExpression RunnerArch { get; } Property Value RawExpression RunnerDebug This is set only if debug logging is enabled, and always has the value of 1. It can be useful as an indicator to enable additional debugging or verbose logging in your own job steps. public RawExpression RunnerDebug { get; } Property Value RawExpression RunnerEnvironment The environment of the runner executing the job. Possible values are: github-hosted for GitHub-hosted runners provided by GitHub, and self-hosted for self-hosted runners configured by the repository owner. public RawExpression RunnerEnvironment { get; } Property Value RawExpression RunnerName The name of the runner executing the job. This name may not be unique in a workflow run as runners at the repository and organization levels could use the same name. public RawExpression RunnerName { get; } Property Value RawExpression RunnerOs The operating system of the runner executing the job. Possible values are Linux, Windows, or macOS. public RawExpression RunnerOs { get; } Property Value RawExpression RunnerTemp The path to a temporary directory on the runner. This directory is emptied at the beginning and end of each job. Note that files will not be removed if the runner's user account does not have permission to delete them. public RawExpression RunnerTemp { get; } Property Value RawExpression RunnerToolCache The path to the directory containing preinstalled tools for GitHub-hosted runners. public RawExpression RunnerToolCache { get; } Property Value RawExpression Secrets This context is the same for each job in a workflow run. You can access this context from any step in a job. This object contains all the properties listed below. public RawExpression Secrets { get; } Property Value RawExpression SecretsGithubToken Automatically created token for each workflow run. public RawExpression SecretsGithubToken { get; } Property Value RawExpression Steps This context changes for each step in a job. You can access this context from any step in a job. This object contains all the properties listed below. public RawExpression Steps { get; } Property Value RawExpression Strategy This context changes for each job in a workflow run. You can access this context from any job or step in a workflow. This object contains all the properties listed below. public RawExpression Strategy { get; } Property Value RawExpression StrategyFailFast When this evaluates to true, all in-progress jobs are canceled if any job in a matrix fails. public RawExpression StrategyFailFast { get; } Property Value RawExpression StrategyJobIndex The index of the current job in the matrix. Note: This number is a zero-based number. The first job's index in the matrix is 0. public RawExpression StrategyJobIndex { get; } Property Value RawExpression StrategyJobTotal The total number of jobs in the matrix. Note: This number is not a zero-based number. For example, for a matrix with four jobs, the value of job-total is 4. public RawExpression StrategyJobTotal { get; } Property Value RawExpression StrategyMaxParallel The maximum number of jobs that can run simultaneously when using a matrix job strategy. public RawExpression StrategyMaxParallel { get; } Property Value RawExpression Vars The vars context contains custom configuration variables set at the organization, repository, and environment levels. public RawExpression Vars { get; } Property Value RawExpression Methods EnvVar(string) The value of a specific environment variable. public RawExpression EnvVar(string envName) Parameters envName string The name of the environment variable. Returns RawExpression InputsInput(string) Each input value passed from an external workflow. public RawExpression InputsInput(string inputName) Parameters inputName string The name of the input. Returns RawExpression JobServicesId(string) The ID of the service container. public RawExpression JobServicesId(string serviceId) Parameters serviceId string The service container ID. Returns RawExpression JobServicesNetwork(string) The ID of the service container network. The runner creates the network used by all containers in a job. public RawExpression JobServicesNetwork(string serviceId) Parameters serviceId string The service container ID. Returns RawExpression JobServicesPort(string, int) A specific exposed port of the service container. public RawExpression JobServicesPort(string serviceId, int port) Parameters serviceId string The service container ID. port int The port number. Returns RawExpression JobServicesPorts(string) The exposed ports of the service container. public RawExpression JobServicesPorts(string serviceId) Parameters serviceId string The service container ID. Returns RawExpression JobsOutput(string, string) The value of a specific output for a job in a reusable workflow. public RawExpression JobsOutput(string jobId, string outputName) Parameters jobId string The job ID. outputName string The output name. Returns RawExpression JobsOutputs(string) The set of outputs of a job in a reusable workflow. public RawExpression JobsOutputs(string jobId) Parameters jobId string The job ID. Returns RawExpression JobsResult(string) The result of a job in the reusable workflow. Possible values are success, failure, cancelled, or skipped. public RawExpression JobsResult(string jobId) Parameters jobId string The job ID. Returns RawExpression MatrixProperty(string) The value of a matrix property. public RawExpression MatrixProperty(string propertyName) Parameters propertyName string The name of the matrix property. Returns RawExpression NeedsJob(string) A single job that the current job depends on. public RawExpression NeedsJob(string jobId) Parameters jobId string The job ID. Returns RawExpression NeedsOutput(string, string) The value of a specific output for a job that the current job depends on. public RawExpression NeedsOutput(string jobId, string outputName) Parameters jobId string The job ID. outputName string The output name. Returns RawExpression NeedsOutputs(string) The set of outputs of a job that the current job depends on. public RawExpression NeedsOutputs(string jobId) Parameters jobId string The job ID. Returns RawExpression NeedsResult(string) The result of a job that the current job depends on. Possible values are success, failure, cancelled, or skipped. public RawExpression NeedsResult(string jobId) Parameters jobId string The job ID. Returns RawExpression SecretsSecret(string) The value of a specific secret. public RawExpression SecretsSecret(string secretName) Parameters secretName string The name of the secret. When a continue-on-error step fails, the outcome is failure, but the final conclusion is success. public RawExpression StepsConclusion(string stepId) Parameters stepId string The step ID. When a continue-on-error step fails, the outcome is failure, but the final conclusion is success. public RawExpression StepsOutcome(string stepId) Parameters stepId string The step ID. api/DecSm.Atom.Module.GithubWorkflows.Gi.title: Class Github.Variables | Atom

[PublicAPI] public interface IGithubReleaseHelper : IGithubHelper, IBuildAccessor Inherited Members IGithubHelper.GithubToken IBuildAccessor.Services IBuildAccessor.Logger IBuildAccessor.AtomFileSystem IBuildAccessor.ProcessRunner IBuildAccessor.GetService<T>() IBuildAccessor.GetServices<T>() IBuildAccessor.GetParam<T>(Expression<Func<T>>, T, Func<string, T>) Remarks This interface extends IGithubHelper to provide functionality for uploading artifacts to a GitHub Release, leveraging the GitHub API. Methods UploadArtifactToRelease(string, string, bool) Uploads a build artifact to a specified GitHub Release. [PublicAPI] Task UploadArtifactToRelease(string artifactName, string releaseTag, bool dryRunWhenNotRunningInGithubActions = true) Parameters artifactName string The name of the artifact to upload. This name should correspond to a directory within the Atom artifacts directory. releaseTag string The tag of the GitHub Release to which the artifact should be uploaded (e.g., "v1.0.0"). dryRunWhenNotRunningInGithubActions bool If true (default), the upload operation will be simulated (logged but not executed) when the build is not running in a GitHub Actions environment. Returns Task A System.Threading.Tasks.Task representing the asynchronous operation. Remarks The artifact will be zipped before uploading if it's a directory. The method will attempt to find an existing release with the given tag. UploadAssetToRelease(string, RootedPath, bool) Uploads a generic asset (file or directory) to a specified GitHub Release. [PublicAPI] Task UploadAssetToRelease(string releaseTag, RootedPath assetPath, bool dryRunWhenNotRunningInGithubActions = true) Parameters releaseTag string The tag of the GitHub Release to which the asset should be uploaded (e.g., "v1.0.0"). assetPath RootedPath The path to the asset (file or directory) to upload. dryRunWhenNotRunningInGithubActions bool If true (default), the upload operation will be simulated (logged but not executed) when the build is not running in a GitHub Actions environment. Returns Task A System.Threading.Tasks.Task representing the asynchronous operation. Remarks If assetPath points to a directory, it will be zipped into a .zip file with the same name before uploading. If it points to a file that is not a .zip file, it will also be zipped. Exceptions InvalidOperationException Thrown if the GitHub repository ID cannot be parsed.

api/DecSm.Atom.Module.GithubWorkflows.He.summary: Interface IGithubWorkflows Namespace DecSm.Atom.Module.GithubWorkflows.Helpers Assembly DecSm.Atom.Module.GithubWorkflows.dll Provides integration with GitHub Actions workflows for DecSm.Atom builds. [PublicAPI] public interface IGithubWorkflows : IJobRunsOn, IBuildAccessor Inherited Members IJobRunsOn.JobRunsOn IBuildAccessor.Services IBuildAccessor.Logger IBuildAccessor.AtomFileSystem IBuildAccessor.ProcessRunner IBuildAccessor.GetService<T>() IBuildAccessor.GetServices<T>() IBuildAccessor.GetParam<T>(Expression<Func<T>>, T, Func<string, T>) Remarks Implementing this interface configures the necessary services for generating GitHub Actions workflow files, providing GitHub-specific workflow variables, and adapting build paths and reporting when running within GitHub Actions. Methods ConfigureBuilder(IHostApplicationBuilder) Configures the host builder to add GitHub Actions workflow services. [UsedImplicitly] protected static void ConfigureBuilder(IHostApplicationBuilder builder) Parameters builder IHostApplicationBuilder The host application builder. Remarks This method registers DecSm.Atom.Module.GithubWorkflows.GithubActions.GithubWorkflowFileWriter and DependabotConfigFileWriter for generating workflow files, and DecSm.Atom.Module.GithubWorkflows.GithubVariableProvider for GitHub-specific workflow variables. When running inside GitHub Actions, it also configures verbose logging, sets up DecSm.Atom.Module.GithubWorkflows.GithubSummaryOutcomeReportWriter for reporting, and adjusts artifact and publish paths to GitHub Actions conventions.

api/DecSm.Atom.Process.IProcessRunner.ht.title: Interface IProcessRunner | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Process.IProcessRunner.ht.summary: Interface IProcessRunner Namespace DecSm.Atom.Process Assembly DecSm.Atom.Process.dll Defines a service for executing external processes with comprehensive logging, output capture, and error handling. [PublicAPI] public interface IProcessRunner Remarks Both stdout and stderr are captured in real time via async read loops so that long-running processes stream their output to the logger rather than buffering it until exit. This interface is registered as a singleton by ProcessHostExtensions. Inject it into build targets and helpers instead of using System.Diagnostics.Process directly, to benefit from consistent logging and uniform error handling across all process invocations. Methods Run(ProcessRunOptions) Executes an external process synchronously and returns its result. ProcessRunResult Run(ProcessRunOptions options) Parameters options ProcessRunOptions The configuration for the process execution. Returns ProcessRunResult A ProcessRunResult containing the exit code and captured output. Only returned when the exit code is zero, or when AllowFailedResult is true. Exceptions Exception Thrown when the process exits with a non-zero code and AllowFailedResult is false. The message includes the command name, exit code, and any captured stderr. RunAsync(ProcessRunOptions, CancellationToken) Executes an external process asynchronously and returns its result. Task<ProcessRunResult> RunAsync(ProcessRunOptions options, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters options ProcessRunOptions The configuration for the process execution. cancellationToken CancellationToken A token that cancels the wait for the process to exit. Note: cancelling the token aborts the wait but does not kill the underlying OS process. Returns Task<ProcessRunResult> A task that resolves to a ProcessRunResult when the process exits. Only completes successfully when the exit code is zero, or when AllowFailedResult is true. Exceptions Exception Thrown (on the returned task) when the process exits with a non-zero code and AllowFailedResult is false. OperationCanceledException Thrown when cancellationToken is cancelled before the process exits.

api/DecSm.Atom.Process.ProcessRunOptions.title: Class ProcessRunOptions | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Process.ProcessRunOptions.summary: Class ProcessRunOptions Namespace DecSm.Atom.Process Assembly DecSm.Atom.Process.dll Immutable configuration for a single external-process execution through IProcessRunner. [PublicAPI] public sealed record ProcessRunOptions Inheritance object ProcessRunOptions Remarks All properties have defaults suited to typical build-automation use: invocation is logged at Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LogLevel.Information, stdout at Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LogLevel.Debug, and stderr at Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LogLevel.Warning. A non-zero exit code throws by default so build steps fail fast without requiring callers to check exit codes manually. Both stdout and stderr are fully captured into ProcessRunResult regardless of log level, so callers can inspect the full output even when logs are suppressed. Constructors ProcessRunOptions(string, string) Immutable configuration for a single external-process execution through IProcessRunner. public ProcessRunOptions(string Name, string Args) Parameters Name string The name or path of the executable to run (e.g. "dotnet", "git"). Args string The command-line arguments to pass to the executable as a pre-joined string. Remarks All properties have defaults suited to typical build-automation use: invocation is logged at Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LogLevel.Information, stdout at Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LogLevel.Debug, and stderr at Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LogLevel.Warning. A non-zero exit code throws by default so build steps fail fast without requiring callers to check exit codes manually. Both stdout and stderr are fully captured into ProcessRunResult regardless of log level, so callers can inspect the full output even when logs are suppressed. ProcessRunOptions(string, string[]) Initializes a new instance of ProcessRunOptions from an array of argument tokens, joining them with a single space. public ProcessRunOptions(string Name, string[] Args) Parameters Name string The name or path of the executable to run. Args string[] An array of argument tokens. Empty and whitespace-only entries are removed before joining so that optional arguments can be passed as string.Empty without creating spurious spaces in the final argument string. Properties AllowFailedResult Gets a value indicating whether a non-zero exit code should be tolerated rather than throwing an exception. public bool AllowFailedResult { get; init; } Property Value bool Remarks When false (the default), Run(ProcessRunOptions) and RunAsync(ProcessRunOptions, CancellationToken) throw if the exit code is non-zero, which causes the enclosing build target to fail immediately. Set to true when the caller needs to inspect the result regardless of success — for example, running a linter and deciding separately whether to fail the build. The caller must then check ExitCode explicitly. Args The command-line arguments to pass to the executable as a pre-joined string. public string Args { get; init; } Property Value string EnvironmentVariables Gets the environment variables to inject into the process's environment. public Dictionary<string, string?> EnvironmentVariables { get; init; } Property Value Dictionary<string, string> Remarks Keys are variable names and values are their string values. A null value removes the inherited variable from the child process's environment. Variables not listed here are inherited from the current process unchanged. ErrorLogLevel Gets the log level used when streaming each line of the process's standard error. public LogLevel ErrorLogLevel { get; init; } Property Value LogLevel Remarks Defaults to Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LogLevel.Warning. Like OutputLogLevel, the full stderr is captured into Error irrespective of this value. On a non-zero exit, stderr is additionally promoted to Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LogLevel.Information or higher when ErrorLogLevel is below that threshold. InvocationLogLevel Gets the log level used when logging the process invocation line (e.g. Run: dotnet build --configuration Release). public LogLevel InvocationLogLevel { get; init; } Property Value LogLevel Remarks Defaults to Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LogLevel.Information. Lower this to Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LogLevel.Debug for noisy or frequently-called sub-processes. Name The name or path of the executable to run (e.g. "dotnet", "git"). public string Name { get; init; } Property Value string OutputLogLevel Gets the log level used when streaming each line of the process's standard output. public LogLevel OutputLogLevel { get; init; } Property Value LogLevel Remarks Defaults to Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LogLevel.Debug. The full stdout is still captured into Output regardless of this setting, so callers can inspect it even when debug logging is disabled. Set to Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.LogLevel.None to suppress stdout entirely from the log. TransformError Gets an optional per-line transformation applied to each line of the process's standard error before it is logged and captured. public Func<string, string?>? TransformError { get; init; } Property Value Func<string, string> Remarks Behaves identically to TransformOutput but operates on stderr. Returning null suppresses the line from both the log and Error. TransformOutput Gets an optional per-line transformation applied to each line of the process's standard output before it is logged and captured. public Func<string, string?>? TransformOutput { get; init; } Property Value Func<string, string> Remarks The delegate receives a raw non-null line (null data events are filtered before the delegate is called). Returning null suppresses the line entirely — it is neither logged nor included in Output. This is useful for filtering noisy progress lines or redacting secrets from captured output. WorkingDirectory Gets the working directory for the process execution. public string? WorkingDirectory { get; init; } Property Value string Remarks When null (the default), the process inherits the current application's working directory. Set this when a tool must be run from a specific project or repository root.

api/DecSm.Atom.Process.ProcessRunResult..summary: Class ProcessRunResult Namespace DecSm.Atom.Process Assembly DecSm.Atom.Process.dll Represents the result of a completed external-process execution. [PublicAPI] public sealed record ProcessRunResult Inheritance object ProcessRunResult Remarks Both Output and Error are always populated with whatever the process wrote, regardless of the log levels configured in RunOptions. This means callers can inspect the full output in error-handling logic even when debug logging was disabled during the run. For processes run with AllowFailedResult set to false (the default), this record is only returned when ExitCode is zero — otherwise IProcessRunner throws before returning. Constructors ProcessRunResult(ProcessRunOptions, int, string, string) Represents the result of a completed external-process execution. public ProcessRunResult(ProcessRunOptions RunOptions, int ExitCode, string Output, string Error) Parameters RunOptions ProcessRunOptions The options that were used to run the process. ExitCode int The exit code returned by the process upon termination. Output string The complete standard output (stdout) captured from the process, with lines joined by newlines. Error string The complete standard error (stderr) captured from the process, with lines joined by newlines. Remarks Both Output and Error are always populated with whatever the process wrote, regardless of the log levels configured in RunOptions. This means callers can inspect the full output in error-handling logic even when debug logging was disabled during the run. For processes run with AllowFailedResult set to false (the default), this record is only returned when ExitCode is zero — otherwise IProcessRunner throws before returning. Properties Error The complete standard error (stderr) captured from the process, with lines joined by newlines. public string Error { get; init; } Property Value string ExitCode The exit code returned by the process upon termination. public int ExitCode { get; init; } Property Value int Output The complete standard output (stdout) captured from the process, with lines joined by newlines. public string Output { get; init; } Property Value string RunOptions The options that were used to run the process. public ProcessRunOptions RunOptions { get; init; } Property Value ProcessRunOptions

api/DecSm.Atom.SemanticVersion.SemVer.ht.title: Class SemVer | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.SemanticVersion.SemVer.ht.summary: Class SemVer Namespace DecSm.Atom.SemanticVersion Assembly DecSm.Atom.SemanticVersion.dll Represents a semantic version following the Semantic Versioning 2.0.0 specification. Supports parsing, comparison, and conversion operations for version strings in the format MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH[-PRERELEASE][+METADATA]. [PublicAPI] public sealed class SemVer Inheritance object SemVer Examples var version1 = SemVer.Parse("1.2.3-alpha.1+build.123"); var version2 = new SemVer("2.0.0"); bool isNewer = version2 > version1; // true Remarks This class implements semantic version comparison where: Release versions (without pre-release) are greater than pre-release versions Pre-release versions are compared lexicographically by dot-separated identifiers Numeric identifiers in pre-release are compared numerically Metadata is used only as a final tiebreaker when all other components are equal Supports implicit conversion to/from string and JSON serialization. Constructors SemVer() Represents a semantic version following the Semantic Versioning 2.0.0 specification. Supports parsing, comparison, and conversion operations for version strings in the format MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH[-PRERELEASE][+METADATA]. public SemVer() Examples var version1 = SemVer.Parse("1.2.3-alpha.1+build.123"); var version2 = new SemVer("2.0.0"); bool isNewer = version2 > version1; // true Remarks This class implements semantic version comparison where: Release versions (without pre-release) are greater than pre-release versions Pre-release versions are compared lexicographically by dot-separated identifiers Numeric identifiers in pre-release are compared numerically Metadata is used only as a final tiebreaker when all other components are equal Supports implicit conversion to/from string and JSON serialization. Properties BuildNumberFromMetadata Gets the build number extracted from the metadata. [JsonIgnore] public int BuildNumberFromMetadata { get; } Property Value int The numeric build number if exactly one number is found in the metadata; otherwise, 0. Remarks This property uses ExtractBuildNumber(string?) to parse the metadata string. Only returns a non-zero value if exactly one numeric sequence is present. BuildNumberFromPreRelease Gets the build number extracted from the pre-release identifier. [JsonIgnore] public int BuildNumberFromPreRelease { get; } Property Value int The numeric build number if exactly one number is found in the pre-release; otherwise, 0. Remarks This property uses ExtractBuildNumber(string?) to parse the pre-release string. Only returns a non-zero value if exactly one numeric sequence is present. IsPreRelease Gets a value indicating whether this version is a pre-release version. [JsonIgnore] public bool IsPreRelease { get; } Property Value bool true if this version has a pre-release identifier; otherwise, false. Major Gets the major version number. public int Major { get; } Property Value int The major version number, indicating incompatible API changes. Metadata Gets the build metadata. public string? Metadata { get; } Property Value string The build metadata string, or null if no metadata is present. Remarks Metadata is ignored when determining version precedence but is used as a tiebreaker in comparisons. Minor Gets the minor version number. public int Minor { get; } Property Value int The minor version number, indicating backwards-compatible functionality additions. One Gets a semantic version representing version 1.0.0. public static SemVer One { get; } Property Value SemVer A SemVer instance with Major set to 1, Minor set to 0, and Patch set to 0. Patch Gets the patch version number. public int Patch { get; } Property Value int The patch version number, indicating backwards-compatible bug fixes. PreRelease Gets the pre-release version identifier. public string? PreRelease { get; } Property Value string The pre-release identifier string, or null if this is a release version. Remarks Pre-release identifiers are dot-separated and may contain alphanumeric characters and hyphens. Prefix Gets the version prefix in the format "MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH". [JsonIgnore] public string Prefix { get; } Property Value string A string representing the core version without pre-release or metadata components. Methods CompareTo(SemVer?) Compares the current instance with another SemVer and returns an integer that indicates whether the current instance precedes, follows, or occurs in the same position in the sort order. public int CompareTo(SemVer? other) Parameters other SemVer The SemVer to compare with this instance. Returns int A value that indicates the relative order of the objects being compared: Less than zero if this instance precedes other; Zero if they are equal; Greater than zero if this instance follows other. Remarks Comparison follows Semantic Versioning precedence rules: Compare major, minor, and patch versions numerically Pre-release versions have lower precedence than normal versions Pre-release identifiers are compared lexicographically, with numeric identifiers compared numerically Metadata is used only as a final tiebreaker Equals(SemVer?) Determines whether the specified SemVer is equal to the current instance. public bool Equals(SemVer? other) Parameters other SemVer The SemVer to compare with the current instance. Returns bool true if the specified SemVer is equal to the current instance; otherwise, false. Equals(object?) Determines whether the specified object is equal to the current instance. public override bool Equals(object? obj) Parameters obj object The object to compare with the current instance. Returns bool true if the specified object is equal to the current instance; otherwise, false. ExtractBuildNumber(string?) Extracts a single numeric value from the specified input string. public static int ExtractBuildNumber(string? input) Parameters input string The string to extract a number from. Returns int The numeric value if exactly one number is found; otherwise, 0. Returns 0 if the input is null, empty, whitespace, or contains zero or multiple numbers. Examples ExtractBuildNumber("alpha.123") // returns 123 ExtractBuildNumber("beta") // returns 0 ExtractBuildNumber("1.2.3") // returns 0 (multiple numbers) Remarks This method is used by BuildNumberFromPreRelease and BuildNumberFromMetadata to extract build numbers from version components. FromSystemVersion(Version, bool) Creates a SemVer instance from a System.Version. public static SemVer FromSystemVersion(Version version, bool throwIfContainsRevision = false) Parameters version Version The System.Version to convert. throwIfContainsRevision bool If true, throws an exception when the version has a non-zero revision. Returns SemVer A SemVer with Major, Minor, and Patch (from Build) components. Remarks Maps System.Version Major.Minor.Build to SemVer Major.Minor.Patch. The revision component is ignored unless throwIfContainsRevision is true. Exceptions ArgumentException Thrown when throwIfContainsRevision is true and the version has a non-zero revision. GetHashCode() Serves as the default hash function. public override int GetHashCode() Returns int A hash code for the current object. IsBetween(SemVer, SemVer) Determines whether the current version is between two specified versions (exclusive). public bool IsBetween(SemVer firstBound, SemVer secondBound) Parameters firstBound SemVer The first boundary version. secondBound SemVer The second boundary version. Returns bool true if this version is between the two bounds; otherwise, false. Remarks The method handles bounds in any order. If both bounds are equal, returns true only if this version equals the bounds. Parse(ReadOnlySpan<char>, IFormatProvider?) Parses a span of characters into a value. public static SemVer Parse(ReadOnlySpan<char> s, IFormatProvider? provider) Parameters s ReadOnlySpan<char> The span of characters to parse. provider IFormatProvider An object that provides culture-specific formatting information about s. Returns SemVer The result of parsing s. Exceptions FormatException s is not in the correct format. OverflowException s is not representable by SemVer. Parse(string) Parses a string representation of a semantic version. public static SemVer Parse(string s) Parameters s string The string to parse. Returns SemVer A SemVer equivalent to the version contained in s. Remarks Accepts version strings in the format: MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH[-PRERELEASE][+METADATA] where MAJOR, MINOR, and PATCH are non-negative integers. Exceptions ArgumentException Thrown when s is not a valid semantic version string. Parse(string, IFormatProvider?) Parses a string into a value. public static SemVer Parse(string s, IFormatProvider? provider) Parameters s string The string to parse. provider IFormatProvider An object that provides culture-specific formatting information about s. Returns SemVer The result of parsing s. Exceptions ArgumentNullException s is null. FormatException s is not in the correct format. OverflowException s is not representable by SemVer. ToString() Returns the string representation of this semantic version. public override string ToString() Returns string A string in the format MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH[-PRERELEASE][+METADATA], where the pre-release and metadata components are included only if present. Examples new SemVer { Major = 1, Minor = 2, Patch = 3 }.ToString() // "1.2.3" new SemVer { Major = 1, Minor = 2, Patch = 3, PreRelease = "alpha" }.ToString() // "1.2.3-alpha" new SemVer { Major = 1, Minor = 2, Patch = 3, Metadata = "build.1" }.ToString() // "1.2.3+build.1" ToSystemVersion(bool, bool) Converts this semantic version to a System.Version instance. public Version ToSystemVersion(bool throwIfContainsPreRelease = false, bool throwIfContainsMetadata = false) Parameters throwIfContainsPreRelease bool If true, throws an exception when pre-release data is present. throwIfContainsMetadata bool If true, throws an exception when metadata is present. Returns Version A System.Version with Major, Minor, and Build (from Patch) components. Remarks Maps SemVer Major.Minor.Patch to System.Version Major.Minor.Build. Pre-release and metadata information is lost in the conversion. Exceptions ArgumentException Thrown when throwIfContainsPreRelease is true and pre-release data is present, or when throwIfContainsMetadata is true and metadata is present. TryParse(ReadOnlySpan<char>, out SemVer) Tries to parse a span of characters representing a semantic version using the invariant culture. public static bool TryParse(ReadOnlySpan<char> s, out SemVer result) Parameters s ReadOnlySpan<char> The span of characters to parse. result SemVer When this method returns, contains the SemVer equivalent to the version contained in s, if the conversion succeeded, or null if the conversion failed. Returns bool true if s was converted successfully; otherwise, false. TryParse(ReadOnlySpan<char>, IFormatProvider?, out SemVer) Tries to parse a span of characters into a value. public static bool TryParse(ReadOnlySpan<char> s, IFormatProvider? provider, out SemVer result) Parameters s ReadOnlySpan<char> The span of characters to parse. provider IFormatProvider An object that provides culture-specific formatting information about s. result SemVer When this method returns, contains the result of successfully parsing s, or an undefined value on failure. Returns bool true if s was successfully parsed; otherwise, false. TryParse(string?, out SemVer) Tries to parse a string representation of a semantic version using the invariant culture. public static bool TryParse(string? s, out SemVer result) Parameters s string The string to parse. result SemVer When this method returns, contains the SemVer equivalent to the version contained in s, if the conversion succeeded, or null if the conversion failed. Returns bool true if s was converted successfully; otherwise, false. TryParse(string?, IFormatProvider?, out SemVer) Tries to parse a string into a value. public static bool TryParse(string? s, IFormatProvider? provider, out SemVer result) Parameters s string The string to parse. provider IFormatProvider An object that provides culture-specific formatting information about s. result SemVer When this method returns, contains the result of successfully parsing s or an undefined value on failure. Returns bool true if s was successfully parsed; otherwise, false. Operators operator ==(SemVer?, SemVer?) Compares two values to determine equality. public static bool operator ==(SemVer? left, SemVer? right) Parameters left SemVer The value to compare with right. right SemVer The value to compare with left. Returns bool true if left is equal to right; otherwise, false. operator >(SemVer, SemVer) Compares two values to determine which is greater. public static bool operator >(SemVer left, SemVer right) Parameters left SemVer The value to compare with right. right SemVer The value to compare with left. Returns bool true if left is greater than right; otherwise, false. operator >=(SemVer, SemVer) Compares two values to determine which is greater or equal. public static bool operator >=(SemVer left, SemVer right) Parameters left SemVer The value to compare with right. right SemVer The value to compare with left. Returns bool true if left is greater than or equal to right; otherwise, false. implicit operator string(SemVer) Converts a SemVer to its string representation. public static implicit operator string(SemVer semVer) Parameters semVer SemVer The SemVer to convert. Returns string The string representation of the semantic version. implicit operator SemVer(string) Converts the string representation of a semantic version to its SemVer equivalent. public static implicit operator SemVer(string s) Parameters s string The string representation of the semantic version. Returns SemVer A SemVer object equivalent to the version contained in s. Exceptions ArgumentException Thrown when s is not a valid semantic version string. operator !=(SemVer?, SemVer?) Compares two values to determine inequality. public static bool operator !=(SemVer? left, SemVer? right) Parameters left SemVer The value to compare with right. right SemVer The value to compare with left. Returns bool true if left is not equal to right; otherwise, false. operator <(SemVer, SemVer) Compares two values to determine which is less. public static bool operator <(SemVer left, SemVer right) Parameters left SemVer The value to compare with right. right SemVer The value to compare with left. Returns bool true if left is less than right; otherwise, false. operator <=(SemVer, SemVer) Compares two values to determine which is less or equal. public static bool operator <=(SemVer left, SemVer right) Parameters left SemVer The value to compare with right. right SemVer The value to compare with left. Returns bool true if left is less than or equal to right; otherwise, false.

Supports glob patterns. public IReadOnlyList<string> IncludedPaths { get; init; } Property Value IReadOnlyList<string> Types Gets the list of pull request event types that should activate this trigger (e.g., "opened", "synchronize"). public IReadOnlyList<string> Types { get; init; } Property Value IReadOnlyList<string>

[PublicAPI] public sealed record WorkflowSecretInjectionForSecretProvider : IBuildOption Inheritance object WorkflowSecretInjectionForSecretProvider Implements IBuildOption Examples // Inject a secret for an Azure Key Vault provider var vaultClientSecret = WorkflowSecretsSecretInjection.Create("AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET"); // Add to workflow configuration var workflowDefinition = new WorkflowDefinition().WithAddedOptions(vaultClientSecret); Remarks This option is similar to WorkflowSecretInjection but prevents ISecretsProvider instances from attempting to look up the secret value themselves. This is crucial for scenarios where the secrets provider itself requires a secret (e.g., a vault access token) to function, avoiding circular dependencies or infinite loops during secret resolution. api/DecSm.Atom.Workflows.Options.UseCust.summary: Class UseCustomArtifactProvider Namespace DecSm.Atom.Workflows.Options Assembly DecSm.Atom.Workflows.dll A workflow option that enables a custom IArtifactProvider for artifact management. [PublicAPI] public sealed record UseCustomArtifactProvider : ToggleBuildOption, IBuildOption Inheritance object ToggleBuildOption UseCustomArtifactProvider Implements IBuildOption Inherited Members ToggleBuildOption.Enabled Examples To enable a custom artifact provider in a workflow: // In your build definition: public override IReadOnlyList<WorkflowDefinition> Workflows => [ new("MyWorkflow") { Options = [UseCustomArtifactProvider.Enabled], // ... other workflow configurations } ]; Remarks When Enabled, the workflow will use the StoreArtifact and RetrieveArtifact targets, which delegate to the registered IArtifactProvider implementation. If this option is disabled, the framework defaults to the native artifact capabilities of the CI/CD platform (e.g., actions/upload-artifact in GitHub Actions). Enabling this option ensures a consistent artifact management strategy across different CI/CD platforms and local builds. See Also IArtifactProvider IStoreArtifact IRetrieveArtifact

api/DecSm.Atom.Workflows.WorkflowOutdate.title: Class WorkflowOutdatedException | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Workflows.WorkflowOutdate.summary: Class WorkflowOutdatedException Namespace DecSm.Atom.Workflows Assembly DecSm.Atom.Workflows.dll Thrown when workflow files are out of date in headless mode. [PublicAPI] [Serializable] public class WorkflowOutdatedException : AtomException Inheritance object Exception AtomException WorkflowOutdatedException Examples Typical CI/CD failure scenario and resolution: // In CI/CD pipeline, the build fails with: // "One or more workflows are out of date. To regenerate workflows, run the build with the --gen flag." // To fix locally: // 1. Run: dotnet run --project ./Build --gen // 2. Commit the regenerated workflow files // 3. Push to repository // Example of catching this exception: try { await atomService.RunAsync(); } catch (WorkflowOutdatedException ex) { Console.WriteLine($"Workflows need regeneration: {ex.Message}"); Console.WriteLine("Run the build with the --gen flag to regenerate workflows."); Environment.ExitCode = 3; } Remarks This exception is thrown when the build is running in headless mode (typically in CI/CD environments) and the workflow files are detected to be out of date. In headless mode, the build does not automatically regenerate workflow files to prevent unexpected changes in automated environments. Headless mode is typically enabled with the --headless flag and is used in CI/CD pipelines to ensure reproducibility and prevent unintended modifications to workflow files. To resolve this error, run the build with the --gen flag to regenerate the workflow files, then commit the updated files to your repository. Constructors WorkflowOutdatedException() Initializes a new instance of the WorkflowOutdatedException class. public WorkflowOutdatedException() WorkflowOutdatedException(string) Initializes a new instance of the WorkflowOutdatedException class with a specified error message. public WorkflowOutdatedException(string message) Parameters message string The message that describes the error. WorkflowOutdatedException(string, Exception) Initializes a new instance of the WorkflowOutdatedException class with a specified error message and a reference to the inner exception that is the cause of this exception. public WorkflowOutdatedException(string message, Exception innerException) Parameters message string The error message that explains the reason for the exception. innerException Exception The exception that is the cause of the current exception, or null if no inner exception is specified. See Also AtomException

[PublicAPI] public interface IWorkflowWriter Remarks This interface is part of a strategy pattern where different writers handle different CI/CD platforms. Implementations are typically registered as singletons and are expected to be thread-safe. Properties WorkflowType Gets the type of workflow that this writer can handle (e.g., GitHub Actions, Azure DevOps). Type WorkflowType { get; } Property Value Type Methods CheckForOutdatedWorkflow(WorkflowModel, CancellationToken) Checks if the existing workflow file is outdated and needs to be regenerated. Task<bool> CheckForOutdatedWorkflow(WorkflowModel workflow, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters workflow WorkflowModel The workflow model to compare against the existing file. cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token. Returns Task<bool> A task that resolves to true if the workflow file is missing or outdated; otherwise, false. Generate(WorkflowModel, CancellationToken) Generates a workflow file from the specified workflow model. Task Generate(WorkflowModel workflow, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters workflow WorkflowModel The workflow model to generate. cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token. Returns Task A task representing the asynchronous generation operation.

[PublicAPI] public abstract class WorkflowFileWriter<T> : IWorkflowWriter<T>, IWorkflowWriter where T : IWorkflowType Type Parameters T The workflow type this writer handles, which must implement IWorkflowType. Inheritance object WorkflowFileWriter<T> Implements IWorkflowWriter<T> IWorkflowWriter Derived DependabotConfigFileWriter DebugWorkflowWriter Remarks This class provides a template for writing structured workflow files, handling file I/O, change detection, and indentation. Concrete implementations must override WriteWorkflow(WorkflowModel) to define the specific file format and FileExtension to specify the file extension. Constructors WorkflowFileWriter(IAtomFileSystem, ILogger<WorkflowFileWriter<T>>) An abstract base class for generating platform-specific workflow files (e.g., for GitHub Actions or Azure DevOps). protected WorkflowFileWriter(IAtomFileSystem atomFileSystem, ILogger<WorkflowFileWriter<T>> logger) Parameters atomFileSystem IAtomFileSystem The file system service for file operations. logger ILogger<WorkflowFileWriter<T>> The logger for diagnostics. Remarks This class provides a template for writing structured workflow files, handling file I/O, change detection, and indentation. Concrete implementations must override WriteWorkflow(WorkflowModel) to define the specific file format and FileExtension to specify the file extension. Properties FileExtension Gets the file extension for the generated workflow file (e.g., "yml"). protected abstract string FileExtension { get; } Property Value string FileLocation Gets the root directory where workflow files should be generated. Defaults to the Atom root directory. protected virtual RootedPath FileLocation { get; } Property Value RootedPath TabSize Gets the number of spaces to use for each indentation level. Defaults to 2. protected virtual int TabSize { get; } Property Value int Methods CheckForOutdatedWorkflow(WorkflowModel, CancellationToken) Checks if the existing workflow file is outdated and needs to be regenerated. public Task<bool> CheckForOutdatedWorkflow(WorkflowModel workflow, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters workflow WorkflowModel The workflow model to compare against the existing file. cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token. Returns Task<bool> true if the workflow file is missing or outdated; otherwise, false. Generate(WorkflowModel, CancellationToken) Generates a workflow file from the provided model, writing it only if the content has changed. public Task Generate(WorkflowModel workflow, CancellationToken cancellationToken = default) Parameters workflow WorkflowModel The workflow model to generate. cancellationToken CancellationToken A cancellation token. Returns Task WriteWorkflow(WorkflowModel) When overridden in a derived class, writes the content of the workflow file using the provided helper methods. protected abstract string WriteWorkflow(WorkflowModel workflow) Parameters workflow WorkflowModel The workflow model to write. Returns string

api/DecSm.Atom.Workflows.Writer.html.title: Namespace DecSm.Atom.Workflows.Writer | Atom
api/DecSm.Atom.Workflows.Writer.html.summary: Namespace DecSm.Atom.Workflows.Writer Classes WorkflowFileWriter<T> An abstract base class for generating platform-specific workflow files (e.g., for GitHub Actions or Azure DevOps). Interfaces IWorkflowWriter Defines a contract for writers that can generate and validate platform-specific workflow files. IWorkflowWriter<T> Provides a strongly-typed version of IWorkflowWriter for a specific workflow type.
